project.name: easy-tdx
project.description: easy-tdx is an open-source Python toolkit for accessing TongDaXin market data over TCP, with indicators, backtesting, a CLI, REST API, and Vue web UI.
project.readmeHead: # easy-tdx [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](LICENSE) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/easy-tdx.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/easy-tdx/) [![GitHub Repo stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/handsomejustin/easy-tdx?style=social)](https://github.com/handsomejustin/easy-tdx) [![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/handsomejustin/easy-tdx)](https://github.com/handsomejustin/easy-tdx) [![Checked with mypy](http://www.mypy-lang.org/static/mypy_badge.svg)](http://www.mypy-lang.org/) [![Ask DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg)](https://deepwiki.com/handsomejustin/easy_tdx) 量化基金花百万买的毫秒级行情通道，散户连一根日线都要手动截图——这不是技术差距，这是数据霸凌。 easy-tdx 要做的事很简单：**把机构的数据锁砸开，扔到每个普通人桌面。** 它是一个完全免费、无需注册、无需 API Key、纯开源的行**情核武器**。 一行命令，A股、港股、美股、期货——K线、报价、资金流向、板块轮动、分时明细、逐笔成交，**毫秒级拉满**。 **34个技术指标**（MACD、KDJ、RSI、BOLL……连”捉妖大师”和”30日乖离率信号”都给你算好）开箱即用。 **缠论分析**（笔、中枢、买卖点、背驰）一键出结果——你不再需要手画分型、猜线段。 **内置回测引擎**——写个策略文件，一行命令跑回测，18 个经典策略自带，多因子组合、策略选股扫描，批量对比哪个最赚钱一目了然。 **回测可视化 Web UI**（v1.17 新增）——Vue3 + ECharts 单页应用，浏览器里选标的、挑策略、调参数，K 线买卖点、净值回撤、19 项绩效指标一目了然。支持组合回测、参数网格寻优、多策略结果对比，**还能把好策略存进策略库（SQLite 持久化），勾选多个策略做资金分仓组合回测看综合表现**，全程零代码。 **数据评级系统**（v1.17.14 新增）——回测结果顶部直接显示 **S/A/B/C/D 五档评级徽章**，1 秒判断「这个品种适不适合经常参与」。评级**不看收益率**（避免被近期大涨误导），只看风险调整后的持有体验：卡玛比率、最大回撤、胜率、利润因子、夏普、波动率六维加权 + 一票否决（系统亏损/深回撤/低胜率直接低评）。京东方那种「收益 126% 但胜率 35%、回撤 41%」的案例会评 **D 档**——明确告诉普通人「别碰，套牢后回本极难」。三个入口（单标的/组合/寻优）都有评级，长线低频策略不会被冤枉（交易少时只降权胜率维

nodes.name: CI
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow running on push and pull_request to main. Three independent jobs: a matrix test job (ubuntu-latest + windows-latest × Python 3.10/3.12/3.13 with pytest + coverage ≥60% and ruff lint/format checks), a mypy static type-checking job (ubuntu-latest, Python 3.13), and a frontend build job (ubuntu-latest, runs npm ci + npm run build in web-ui to enforce type-check + vite build as a regression guard after the v1.17.9 frontend numerical bug). Builds web-ui/dist first because pyproject.toml force-includes it into the wheel via hatchling.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML GitHub Actions workflow. Uses `matrix` strategy (os × python-version) with `fail-fast: false`. Triggers on push/PR to `main`. Pins action versions (`actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-python@v5`, `actions/setup-node@v4`) and uses `actions/setup-node` cache keyed on `web-ui/package-lock.json` for fast npm install. Working-directory is set to `web-ui` for npm steps so the cache-dependency-path and working directory align.

nodes.name: Publish to PyPI
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes the package to PyPI when a version tag (v*) is pushed. Runs on ubuntu-latest in the `release` GitHub environment with OIDC trusted publishing (`id-token: write` + `attestations: true` for sigstore provenance). Steps: checkout → setup-python 3.13 → setup-node 20 → npm ci + npm run build (web-ui) → pip install build → python -m build → pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1. Building the frontend before `python -m build` is required because hatchling force-includes web-ui/dist into the wheel so `pip install easy-tdx[web]` ships a working UI.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML GitHub Actions workflow. Triggers exclusively on `tags: [v*]` (no manual dispatch). Uses OIDC `id-token: write` permission so `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` can publish via PyPI trusted publishing without API tokens; `attestations: true` enables PEP 740 sigstore provenance so end users can verify artifact origin.

nodes.name: Release EXE
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds a Windows single-file EXE via PyInstaller and publishes it as a GitHub Release asset when a `v*` tag is pushed. Two jobs: `build-windows` (windows-latest, Python 3.12, Node 20, builds web-ui, `pip install -e .[web,packaging]`, runs `pyinstaller easy_tdx.spec --noconfirm`, renames the output to `easy-tdx-${VERSION}-windows.exe`, uploads as artifact) and `release` (ubuntu-latest, downloads the artifact, creates a GitHub Release via softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with auto-generated notes and a Chinese user-facing download guide that explains the SmartScreen bypass). Phase 1 produces unsigned binaries; Phase 2 will add code signing.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML GitHub Actions workflow with two jobs and explicit `needs: build-windows` dependency. Sets `permissions: contents: write` so softprops/action-gh-release can create the release. The release body (Chinese) explains the unsigned-binary SmartScreen flow to elderly end users. The job order matters: frontend build → `pip install -e .[web,packaging]` (force-include requires dist) → pyinstaller → rename via shell script.

nodes.name: README
nodes.summary: Primary project README (1823 lines) describing easy-tdx as a free, no-API-key TCP-based TongDaXin market data client for A-shares, HK/US stocks, and futures. Covers the three installation modes (PyPI wheel, editable source, packaged EXE), the full CLI surface (kline, quote, tick, transaction, board, capital-flow, indicator, chanlun, backtest, screen, offline, serve), Python API entry points (TdxClient, MacClient, ExTdxClient, MacExClient, UnifiedTdxClient), the 18-strategy backtest engine, 19-factor portfolio manager, S/A/B/C/D grading system (v1.17.14), Vue3 web UI architecture (single-symbol/portfolio/optimize/compare/strategies/server-settings pages), the offline vipdoc read+sync subsystem, and full API tables for all four client families. Includes CLI command summary, enumeration reference, complete API list, module architecture diagram, development setup, credits, and disclaimer.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown document. Uses Chinese throughout for prose (the primary user language), with English code/CLI/API names. Includes fenced code blocks for shell, python, json, ini. Major sections (## headers): 安装, CLI 参考, Python API, 公告检索, 财报三表, 通达信原生 F10, 扩展市场, 离线数据, Web API, CLI 命令汇总, 枚举参考, 完整 API 列表, 架构, 开发, 致谢, 更新日志, 免责声明. Documents the v1.20.0 release line at the top with feature highlights.

nodes.name: Changelog
nodes.summary: Version history for easy-tdx (1024 lines, format: Keep a Changelog zh-CN) covering releases from 1.20.0 (2026-07-08, automatic failover/ping-switch on connection failure) backwards through 1.20 → 1.9.10. Each release block names a one-line headline change, then lists 新增 (Added), 修复 (Fixed), 变更 (Changed), 已知约束 (Known constraints) sections with explicit file paths and test counts. Recent highlights: v1.20.0 unified auto-failover for 8 clients in `_reconnect.py`, v1.19.7 server settings UI + hot-reconnect, v1.19.5 frontend dist packed into wheel via hatchling force-include, v1.18.x backtest visual web UI + strategy library, v1.17.14 S/A/B/C/D grading, v1.17.x 18 builtin strategies + multi-strategy combo, v1.17.2 QFQ negative-price client-side recompute, v1.16.2 quality hardening (3 rounds of code audit), v1.15.4 ETF/指数 3-decimal price parsing.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown document in Chinese following Keep a Changelog zh-CN format. Each release entry uses ## [version] — date heading, with the headline change as bold one-liner, followed by sub-sections (### 新增/### 修复/### 变更/### 已知约束/### 说明/### 文档/### 发布工程). Recent releases consistently cite file paths (src/easy_tdx/_reconnect.py, web-ui/src/views/ServerSettingsView.vue, etc.), regression test counts, and quality metrics (单测数量, ruff/mypy/vue-tsc passes).

nodes.name: Welcome Discussion
nodes.summary: GitHub Discussions welcome message (46 lines) framed around the project's mission to 'break down financial data barriers so anyone can access the same market data as institutions'. Outlines five engagement channels (ask questions, share strategies, file bugs, propose features, showcase projects), gives a quickstart with `pip install easy-tdx` followed by three sample commands (kline, indicator, serve), lists community norms (mutual respect, version + command + error in bug reports, no investment advice), and links to README/Wiki.
nodes.languageNotes: Short markdown document, Chinese prose with English code commands. Uses emoji headers (👋 💬 🚀 🤝). Single-page intro — no deep structure.

nodes.name: Dev requirements lock
nodes.summary: Pinned dev-toolchain lock file (27 lines) locking exact versions of pytest 9.0.3, pytest-asyncio>=0.23, pytest-cov 7.1.0, coverage 7.14.0, mypy 2.1.0, ruff 0.11.11, scipy 1.15.3 (3.10-compatible upper bound), numpy 2.2.3, fastapi 0.115.11, uvicorn 0.34.0. Header comment explains the version-compat rationale (scipy 1.16+ requires Python 3.11, but CI matrix includes 3.10) and notes that runtime dependencies are bounded separately in pyproject.toml.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain-text pip requirements format. Header is a multi-line comment (lines 1-8) explaining the locking policy; the rest is package==version entries grouped by comment headings (测试框架 / 静态检查 / 科学计算 / Web 可选依赖测试支持).

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Build + project manifest (85 lines) for easy-tdx 1.20.0 using hatchling as build backend. Declares runtime deps (pandas>=2.0,<3, tzdata>=2024.1, click>=8.0,<9), CLI entry point `easy-tdx` → `easy_tdx.cli:cli`, and optional extras (dev: pytest+asyncio+cov+mypy+ruff+scipy+httpx; science: scipy; web: fastapi+uvicorn; packaging: pystray+Pillow). Hatch config force-includes `web-ui/dist` into `easy_tdx/web/dist` so wheel installs ship a working UI; mypy strict + ruff E/F/I/UP enforced; pytest auto-asyncio mode; coverage gate raised to 60 (audit #9).
nodes.languageNotes: TOML with PEP 621 [project], [tool.hatch.build], [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel], [tool.mypy] + [[tool.mypy.overrides]], [tool.ruff] + [tool.ruff.lint], [tool.pytest.ini_options], [tool.coverage.run] + [tool.coverage.report]. Force-include mapping is the key cross-cutting contract between frontend build (npm run build) and wheel install.

nodes.name: Read the Docs config
nodes.summary: Read the Docs v2 configuration (18 lines) pinning ubuntu-22.04 + Python 3.11 build environment, pointing Sphinx at `docs/conf.py` for configuration, requesting PDF + EPUB output formats, and installing docs/requirements.txt (sphinx, sphinx-autobuild, myst-parser, sphinx-rtd-theme) before the project itself.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML key:value file. The schema version 2 directives are: build (os, tools.python), sphinx.configuration, formats list, python.install.requirements. No project install step — Sphinx builds the docs/ tree from Markdown via MyST.

nodes.name: Docs requirements
nodes.summary: Pinned sphinx stack (4 lines) used by Read the Docs and local docs builds: sphinx>=7.0, sphinx-autobuild>=2024.0, myst-parser>=3.0 (Markdown source), sphinx-rtd-theme>=2.0.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain-text pip requirements format. Lower-bound only (no upper caps), consistent with docs tooling moving faster than runtime libs.

nodes.name: 2026-06-09-backtest-engine.md
nodes.summary: Backtest Engine implementation plan (2361 lines) describing the bottom-up construction of the new easy_tdx.backtest package: Signal/Trade/Position/BacktestResult dataclasses, a Strategy ABC with a StrategyDataProxy indirection, an OrderSimulator supporting multiple execution rules and a rejection strategy, a PortfolioTracker for positions and equity, a PerformanceAnalyzer delivering 18 metrics, and a BacktestEngine orchestrating vectorised execution. It also stubs out a @dsl_strategy decorator, adds a Click-based `easy-tdx backtest` sub-command, and includes TDD-style test files for types, strategy, orders, portfolio, performance, engine, and CLI. The plan targets the design spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-backtest-engine-design.md (rev 2.1) and is implemented in pure-Python (pandas + numpy + click) with zero network dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Long bilingual (English headings, Chinese narrative) Markdown using checkbox task lists, fenced Python code blocks, file-path tables, and progressive TDD instructions (write failing test → implement → commit).

nodes.name: 2026-06-12-v1.11.0-factor-engine.md
nodes.summary: v1.11.0 Factor Engine implementation plan (1790 lines) that introduces the easy_tdx.factor package: a Factor ABC plus FACTORY_REGISTRY with a register_factor decorator, a FactorEngine supporting compute_single, compute_cross_section, and compute_forward_returns modes, and 15 builtin factors spanning momentum, volatility, quality, volume, technical, ChanLun, and value categories (with pe_ratio/pb_ratio as placeholders). It bridges existing MyTT indicators and ChanlunAnalyser so factor computation reuses prior code, ships a `easy-tdx factor list` (plus category filter and table output) CLI command, and bumps pyproject.toml to 1.11.0. The plan covers only v1.11.0; v1.12.0 analysis/preprocessing and v1.13.0 portfolio are deferred to later plans.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual Markdown mixing Chinese prose with English symbols/CLI flags; uses checkbox progress, fenced Python, file-action tables, and TDD step sequences including pytest assertions.

nodes.name: 2026-06-12-v1.12.0-factor-analysis.md
nodes.summary: v1.12.0 Factor Analysis & Preprocessing plan (877 lines) that layers six pure-function preprocessing routines on top of the v1.11.0 factor package — winsorize (MAD-based clipping), zscore (cross-section or time-series), rank_normalize, fill_missing, orthogonalize, and a top-level preprocess pipeline — plus a FactorAnalyzer offering IC, quantile, turnover, decay, and full_report analyses over long-format [date, code, factor] cross-section data. It adds the `easy-tdx factor analyze` CLI sub-command, wires exports through easy_tdx.factor, and bumps pyproject.toml to 1.12.0. The plan specifies the expected input schemas (factor_data columns date/code/factor_name*, return_data columns date/code/forward_Nd) and references design-spec section 3.4 and 3.5.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual Markdown; tabular file-action lists, fenced Python with class-based pytest skeletons, deterministic-RNG data factories, and ASCII input-schema descriptions.

nodes.name: 2026-06-12-v1.13.0-portfolio.md
nodes.summary: v1.13.0 Portfolio Management plan (956 lines) that completes the factor-to-portfolio closed loop with three layers under easy_tdx.portfolio: a WeightOptimizer ABC plus four registered optimizers (EqualWeightOptimizer, FactorWeightedOptimizer, MeanVarianceOptimizer with scipy-optional degradation, RiskParityOptimizer), a RiskModel covering covariance estimation and portfolio risk, and a RebalanceEngine orchestrating multi-period rebalancing backtests sourced from FactorEngine.compute_cross_section. It defines PortfolioState and RebalanceResult dataclasses, extends the `factor` CLI with a portfolio sub-command, and bumps pyproject.toml to 1.13.0. The plan references design-spec section 4 and keeps each layer independently usable.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual Markdown with file-action tables, dataclass + registry-decorator Python snippets, scoped pytest fixtures (_make_market), and progressive commit messages keyed to each task.

nodes.name: 2026-06-12-v1.14.0-slippage-execution.md
nodes.summary: v1.14.0 Slippage Models and Execution Simulation plan (1724 lines) that extends the backtest engine with pluggable, fully backward-compatible realism. It introduces src/easy_tdx/backtest/slippage.py containing a SlippageModel ABC and four builtins (FixedSlippage, PercentSlippage, SquareRootSlippage, VolumeSlippage) and src/easy_tdx/backtest/execution.py with an ExecutionModel ABC plus ImmediateExecution, TWAPExecution, VWAPExecution, and LimitExecution. The plan modifies orders.py and engine.py to accept the new models via optional parameters so existing APIs and tests remain green, adds ~15 slippage tests and ~20 execution tests, and bumps pyproject.toml to 1.14.0.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual Markdown heavily interspersed with pytest cases per model class, drawing helper functions like _volume_series, and reusing the existing TDD checkbox format with explicit ruff/mypy/test gates.

nodes.name: 2026-06-12-v1.15.0-attribution.md
nodes.summary: v1.15.0 Performance Attribution plan (540 lines) that adds backtest/attribution.py containing an AttributionReport dataclass and an AttributionAnalyzer supporting three attribution views: cost attribution (decomposing commissions, slippage, and stamp tax 0.1% on SELL), Brinson-Hood-Beebower attribution (allocation vs selection vs interaction versus a benchmark), and factor attribution (factor returns plus specific return). The analyzer wires naturally into the existing trade and equity-curve outputs from BacktestEngine and accepts optional factor exposures/returns and group mappings, ships ~20 unit tests, and bumps pyproject.toml to 1.15.0.
nodes.languageNotes: Compact bilingual Markdown focused on a single implementation task plus a closing version-bump task; uses dataclass field defaults, docstring-style method contracts, and TDD step ordering.

nodes.name: Backtest Engine Design Spec
nodes.summary: Approved rev-2 internal design spec (2026-06-09) for the new `easy_tdx.backtest` module — the first piece of the full quantitative toolchain. Defines a pure-compute (pandas/numpy only, no network) vectorized execution pipeline (signal → matching → portfolio → performance) with two strategy modes: Python class inheritance with `init()`/`next()` lifecycle + `StrategyDataProxy` (exposes OHLCV columns plus any precomputed indicator columns via `__getattr__`) and a P1 DSL with both `@dsl_strategy` decorator and CLI string forms (`CROSS(MA(close,5),MA(close,20))`). Core datatypes: `Signal`, `Trade`, `Position`, `BacktestResult`. Engine `run()` accepts an easy-tdx standard K-line DataFrame plus optional `chanlun_result` injected as `self.chanlun`. `OrderSimulator` supports five execution-price rules (next_open default; this_close logs red future-leak warning and sets `BacktestResult.config.future_leak_warning=True`), four position modes (full/fixed/percent/signal_only), and two order-reject policies (reduce/skip). `PerformanceAnalyzer` computes 18 metrics (total/annual return, max_drawdown + duration, sharpe/sortino/calmar, win_rate/profit_factor/avg_win/loss, alpha/information_ratio). CLI integration: `easy-tdx backtest` auto-fetches K-lines, supports `--strategy`/`--strategy-file`/`--params`/`--grid`/`--indicators`/`--table`/`--output csv`. v1 P1 deferrals documented: event-driven execution, multi-symbol portfolio backtest, risk controls, real-time paper trading, visualization.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown design spec for `src/easy_tdx/backtest/` (P0 implementation target). Uses Python pseudocode blocks for dataclasses (`Signal`, `Trade`, `Position`, `BacktestResult`), abstract base class `Strategy`, and engine signature `BacktestEngine(strategy, cash=100000, commission=0.0003, min_commission=5.0, stamp_tax=0.001, slippage=0.0, execution='next_open', position_mode='full', order_reject_policy='reduce')`. Includes ASCII architecture diagram (`backtest/` file tree) and a `Signal → Execution → Portfolio → Performance` interaction diagram. Document is bilingual (Chinese narrative, English code) — has been formally 'Approved (rev 2 — incorporated DeepSeek feasibility review)'.

nodes.name: Advanced Backtest Enhancement Spec
nodes.summary: Internal design spec v1.0 (2026-06-12) for the three-Plan-B enhancements to the existing `BacktestEngine` + `OrderSimulator`: pluggable slippage models, execution simulation, and return attribution. Newly introduces `SlippageModel` ABC in `slippage.py` with four implementations — `FixedSlippage(per_share=0.01)` (backward-compatible), `PercentSlippage(rate=0.001)`, `SquareRootSlippage(impact_coeff=0.1)` (A-share mainstream: `σ × √(Q/V) × price × Q × coeff`, degrades to `PercentSlippage` when vol or volatility is 0), `VolumeSlippage(base_bps=10.0)` (volume-proportional). New `ExecutionModel` ABC in `execution.py` with `ImmediateExecution` (current behavior, backward-compatible), `TWAPExecution(n_bars=5)` (even split + 100-share-lot rounding per child), `VWAPExecution(n_bars=5, volume_lookback=20)` (split by historical volume distribution), `LimitExecution(ttl_bars=5)` (only fills if bar's low≤target for BUY or high≥target for SELL, degrades to immediate if price is None). New `AttributionAnalyzer` in `attribution.py` implements Brinson-Hood-Beebower decomposition (`Allocation + Selection + Interaction`), factor regression (`R = Σ(β_i × f_i) + α`), and cost breakdown (commission/slippage/stamp_tax). `BacktestEngine` gains `slippage_model=` and `execution_model=` kwargs (both nullable for full backward compat); `engine._generate_signals` loop must be rewritten to handle multi-Trade emission from execution models. Ship plan: v1.14.0 (slippage + execution, ~35 tests), v1.15.0 (attribution, ~20 tests). Files explicitly NOT modified: `strategy.py`, `performance.py`, `portfolio.py`, `combo.py`. Out-of-scope: order-book sim (Level-2 unavailable), margin/short, futures/options hedging, HFT.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown design spec extending `src/easy_tdx/backtest/` with three new modules (`slippage.py`, `execution.py`, `attribution.py`) and modifying `engine.py`, `orders.py`, `types.py`. Pseudocode blocks define ABCs with abstract `compute(price, size, volume, volatility, direction) -> float` and `execute(signal, df, bar_idx, cash, position, ...) -> list[Trade]`. Target A-share market only — explicitly excludes margin, index-futures hedging, and order-book simulation.

nodes.name: Quantitative Factor Engine + Portfolio Spec
nodes.summary: Internal design spec v1.0 (2026-06-12) for Plan A — adding factor research infrastructure + portfolio management to easy-tdx as a closed loop 'idea → factor → weighting → rebalance → performance'. New `src/easy_tdx/factor/` package: `base.py` (`Factor` ABC with `name`/`category`/`description`/`inputs`/`compute()` + `@register_factor` decorator mirroring `indicator._REGISTRY`), `engine.py` (`FactorEngine.compute_single`, `compute_cross_section`, `compute_forward_returns`), `analysis.py` (`FactorAnalyzer` with `compute_ic`/`compute_quantile_returns`/`compute_decay` returning `FactorReport` with ic_mean/ir/quantile_returns/top_minus_bottom/turnover_rate/autocorr), `transform.py` (pure functions `winsorize`/`zscore`/`rank_normalize`/`fill_missing`/`orthogonalize` + `preprocess()` one-shot pipeline), and `builtin/` with six category files — momentum/volatility/quality/volume/technical (bridges `indicator.py` via `TechnicalFactor` wrapper) /chanlun (bridges `ChanlunAnalyser` via `ChanlunFactor`). 15-18 initial factors documented (momentum_20d, reversal_5d, volatility_20d, atr_14d, sharpe_20d, max_drawdown_20d, win_rate_20d, obv_trend, vol_surge, amount_ma_ratio, macd_hist_signal, rsi_14, boll_position, chanlun_bi_dir, chanlun_mmd, pe_ratio, pb_ratio). New `src/easy_tdx/portfolio/` package: `optimizer.py` with `EqualWeight/FactorWeighted/RiskParity/MeanVarianceOptimizer` (Markowitz + scipy optional with graceful degradation to equal-weight), `risk.py` (`RiskModel` with Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage covariance + portfolio_risk decomposition), `rebalance.py` (`RebalanceEngine(optimizer, factor_name, n_stocks=50, rebalance_freq='W|M|Q')` producing `RebalanceResult` with multi-period equity_curve). Deliverable cadence: v1.11.0 (factor base + engine + 15 builtins), v1.12.0 (transform + analysis), v1.13.0 (portfolio). Optional dependency: `scipy>=1.10` under extra `factor`. Explicitly NOT adding: cvxpy, sklearn, statsmodels. Bridges `BacktestEngine` via new `factors=` kwarg injecting factor columns into `StrategyDataProxy`.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown design spec for two new top-level packages `src/easy_tdx/factor/` and `src/easy_tdx/portfolio/`. Detailed ASCII file-tree diagrams for both packages. Pseudocode defines `Factor`/`register_factor`/`FACTORY_REGISTRY` (mirrors existing `indicator._REGISTRY` pattern), pipeline data shape `columns=[date, code, factor_1, factor_2, ...]`, dataclasses `PortfolioState`/`RebalanceResult`, and abstractions `WeightOptimizer`/`RiskModel`/`RebalanceEngine`. Single most complex spec in this batch: spans factor research AND portfolio management AND bridges to backtest + indicators + chanlun.

nodes.name: company_info_category.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (7 lines) for the `company_info_category` hex fixture. Records the company-info category index list returned by the 通达信 web API: num_records=16 categories, with the first record pointing to file `600000.txt` (length 11426 bytes) — i.e. the 浦发银行 company-info file has 16 sub-documents and the first is 11426 bytes long. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_company_info_category_parse (line 513) and tests/unit/test_decode_errors.py (line 31) to assert parse_response output for GetCompanyInfoCategoryCmd.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain JSON object with two top-level keys: `num_records` (int) and `first` (object with `filename`, `start`, `length`). The corresponding `.hex` file holds the raw decompressed server body that parse_response consumes; this .json is the human-readable oracle used by the offline tests.

nodes.name: company_info_content.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (3 lines) for the `company_info_content` hex fixture. Records the total byte length (8070) and the 31-byte UTF-8 prefix (`☆最新提示☆ ◇600000 浦发银行 更新日期：2026-04-10◇ 港澳资讯 灵通V9.0\r\n`) that prefixes the raw 灵通V9.0 company-info body so the parser can split header vs content. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_company_info_content_parse (line 535) to validate GetCompanyInfoContentCmd.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON object with `length` (int) and `prefix` (string, contains CRLF). The `prefix` mirrors the exact byte sequence the parser must locate to know where the F10 text body begins; the .hex contains the full decoded body.

nodes.name: ex_history_transaction.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (22 lines) for the Hong-Kong `ex_history_transaction` hex fixture (HK_MAIN_BOARD, market=31, code=00700, 2026-07-03, issue #14 fixture). Encodes 10 historical ticks with integer prices in 0.001 HKD units (price_int=431800 → 431.8 HKD), including first tick at 15:59 (closing-auction small lot, vol=300) and last tick at 16:08 (closing-call-auction block trade, vol=934700). The leading `_comment` block documents the HKD unit convention so future readers understand the integer pricing. Consumed by tests/unit/test_hk_transaction.py (line 57) to verify HK tick parsing on the dedicated extension module.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with leading `_comment` (string), then market/code/num_records + `first` + `last` blocks. The price_int/price_hkd pair is the key teaching artifact: HK tick decoding produces integer minor-units (0.001 HKD) that the public API must divide by 1000. The timestamps cross 16:00 to validate post-close auction handling.

nodes.name: finance_info.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (4 lines) for the `finance_info` hex fixture. Records three core share-structure fields for 600000 浦发银行: `liutong_guben` and `zong_guben` both 33,305,837,500 shares (流通股本 = 总股本, i.e. fully tradable) and `meigujing_zichan` (每股净资产) ≈ 22.13 CNY. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_finance_info_parse (line 487) to validate GetFinanceInfoCmd + models/finance.py decoding of the binary financial-statement body.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with three flat float fields. Float values (`33305837500.0`, `22.1299991607666`) preserve the exact binary-decoded magnitudes — `meigujing_zichan` carries the extra decimals to detect IEEE-754 rounding regressions.

nodes.name: history_minute_time.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (6 lines) for the `history_minute_time` hex fixture (historical full-day 1-minute bars). Reports num_records=240 (a full A-share trading day = 240 one-minute bars) with the first bar at price=10.29, vol=10044. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (line 343) to verify GetHistoryMinuteTimeCmd parses a full session of intraday bars back into a timeseries of (time, price, vol) tuples.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON object with `num_records` (int) and `first` block. The `num_records=240` constant is the canonical A-share trading-day bar count (4h × 60m) and acts as a regression sentinel for the timeseries decoder.

nodes.name: history_transaction.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (8 lines) for the `history_transaction` hex fixture (historical intraday tick distribution). Reports num_records=10 historical ticks, first tick at 14:56 with price=10.3, vol=50. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (line 395) to validate GetHistoryTransactionCmd — used for reconstructing minute-of-day price/volume distributions from a past date.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON object with `num_records` (int) and `first` block containing hour/minute/price/vol. Note the field name is `price` (float), in contrast to ex_history_transaction which uses integer minor-units for HK.

nodes.name: minute_time.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (7 lines) for the current-day `minute_time` hex fixture (1-minute bars for today's live session). Reports num_records=240 bars, first bar at price=0.01, vol=48, with an extra `_unknown_1` field set to 54 (a layout-decoding sentinel — the parser must skip an undocumented uint16/uint32 field without crashing). Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (line 314) to validate GetMinuteTimeCmd + timeseries decoding, including tolerance for the unknown leading field.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with `num_records` (int), `first` (price/vol/_unknown_1). The `_unknown_1` field documents a parser-internal byte the decoder must consume but currently exposes as opaque — useful when regression-introducing fields appear in the protocol.

nodes.name: security_bars.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (9 lines) for the `security_bars` hex fixture (historical K-line / OHLCV bars). Reports num_records=5 bars; first bar is OHLCV = (10.25, 10.25, 10.08, 10.12, 41151820.0 shares). Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (lines 105, 136, 155) to validate GetSecurityBarsCmd against multiple slicing scenarios (partial first record, full 5-record session, boundary handling).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON object with `num_records` (int) and `first` block (open/high/low/close/vol). The vol=41151820.0 float preserves the exact decoded share volume so the offline test can compare equality, not just range.

nodes.name: security_count.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (2 lines) for the `security_count` hex fixture. Reports count=26885 — the recorded number of securities on the SH market at capture time. The body is a fixed 2-byte unsigned int. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_security_count_parse (line 29) to assert GetSecurityCountCmd returns exactly 26885 for the SH market.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal JSON: just `{"count": 26885}`. This is the simplest fixture in the suite and serves as a smoke test that the offline-test harness can load hex bodies at all.

nodes.name: security_list.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (8 lines) for the `security_list` hex fixture (paginated security list page 0). Reports num_records=1000 (a full page), with the first record being code=`999999` / name=`上证指数` / pre_close=3966.171142578125. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (lines 48 and 90) to validate GetSecurityListCmd parses a full page and surfaces the correct first record (the SH composite index).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with `num_records` (int) and `first` block (code, name, pre_close). The `pre_close` value is an exact 32-bit float sentinel (3966.171142578125) used to detect any IEEE-754 rounding drift in the float decoder.

nodes.name: security_quotes.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (8 lines) for the `security_quotes` hex fixture (live snapshot quotes). Reports num_records=1 quote; the record is code=`600000` with `pre_close=9.93`, and two sentinel fields `unknown_2=-1` and `unknown_3=22694` that exercise the parser's tolerance for partially-decoded fields. Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_security_quotes_parse (line 175) to validate GetSecurityQuotesCmd survives unknown byte slots without raising.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with `num_records` (int) and `first` block (code, pre_close, unknown_2, unknown_3). The `unknown_*` keys are the parser-internal byte slots exposed to the offline test — they double as both regression sentinels and a documented placeholder for future protocol fields.

nodes.name: transaction.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (8 lines) for the current-day `transaction` hex fixture (today's tick distribution). Reports num_records=10 ticks; first tick at 14:59 with price=9.9, vol=0 (a zero-volume print at the very end of the regular session). Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (line 365) to validate GetTransactionCmd and confirm zero-volume ticks are tolerated.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with `num_records` (int) and `first` block (hour/minute/price/vol). The vol=0 sentinel is a deliberate edge case for downstream consumers that might divide by vol or compute VWAP.

nodes.name: xdxr_info.json
nodes.summary: Expected-values manifest (8 lines) for the `xdxr_info` hex fixture (除权除息 / ex-dividend & ex-rights event history). Reports num_records=87 events; first event dated 1999-11-10 with category=5 (拆股 / split, by tdx category code). Consumed by tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py (line 423) and tests/unit/test_decode_errors.py (line 39) to validate GetXdxrInfoCmd + the year/month/day/category decoder, including the long historical tail (87 records back to 1999).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with `num_records` (int) and `first` block (year, month, day, category). The category integer maps to a tdx enum (1=送股, 2=配股, 3=转股, 4=派息, 5=拆股, ...) — preserving the integer rather than the enum forces the offline test to also exercise the enum-mapping layer.

nodes.name: company_info_category.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for the GetCompanyInfoCategoryCmd parser. Captures the response size and the position of the first category entry (filename '600000.txt', start offset 0, 11426-byte payload) parsed from `tests/fixtures/company_info_category.hex`. Used by `tests/unit/test_commands_offline.py::test_company_info_category_parse` to assert that the binary parser produces the expected file index structure.

nodes.name: company_info_content.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for the GetCompanyInfoContentCmd parser. Holds the body length (8070) and the visible prefix string (SPDF headers and the 2026-04-10 update stamp) extracted from the matching `company_info_content.hex` blob. Used to verify that the parser preserves length and decodes the GBK-prefixed company narrative correctly.

nodes.name: ex_history_transaction.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for the HK-market `GetExHistoryTransactionDataCmd` parser (ex protocol 0x2406). Captures market=31 (HK_MAIN_BOARD), code 00700, num_records=10, and first/last record timestamps+prices around the 2026-07-03 HK closing auction. Crucially exposes the integer price unit (0.001 HKD, e.g. 431800=HK$431.8) and is anchored to issue #14. Consumed by `tests/unit/test_hk_transaction.py::test_parse_ex_history_transaction_hk` to lock down the HK price-decimal-digits conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON fixture for a HK-specific ex-protocol response; the leading `_comment` key (ignored by `load_json`) documents the protocol quirks (price integer unit, market id, closing-call timestamps).

nodes.name: finance_info.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetFinanceInfoCmd. Captures three representative financial indicators (流通股本/总股本/每股净资产, all aligned to 浦发银行 SH 2026 snapshot) parsed from the matching .hex blob. Used by `test_commands_offline.py` to assert that the float32 / fixed-point decoder yields the expected numerics (33.305B shares, NAV 22.13 CNY).

nodes.name: history_minute_time.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetHistoryMinuteTimeDataCmd. Records num_records=240 (one trading day of 1-minute bars) plus the first parsed bar (price 10.29, volume 10044). Pair file for `history_minute_time.hex`. Used to verify that historical minute K-line decoding produces a full 240-row frame and correct decimal price.

nodes.name: history_transaction.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetHistoryTransactionDataCmd. Captures num_records=10 plus the first record (14:56, price 10.3, vol 50). Pair file for `history_transaction.hex`. Used to validate the historical-tick record shape (timestamp, price, volume) and decimal handling.

nodes.name: minute_time.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetMinuteTimeDataCmd. Records 240 intraday bars plus the first row (price 0.01, vol 48, unknown_1=54). The presence of `_unknown_1` documents known open-question fields flagged by the parser and forwarded untouched to the consumer; used as a regression sentinel so future decoders don't silently lose unknown tail bytes.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains an `_unknown_<n>`-style key. These names match the protocol-decoder convention for fields whose semantics are not yet mapped; tests assert that unknowns are passed through rather than dropped.

nodes.name: security_bars.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetSecurityBarsCmd / GetIndexBarsCmd. Holds num_records=5 plus the canonical OHLCV row of the first K-line bar (open 10.25, high 10.25, low 10.08, close 10.12, vol 41.15M). Pair file for `security_bars.hex`. Used by multiple offline tests including `test_security_bars_parse`, range/shrink-paging tests, and the pre_close float-precision test.

nodes.name: security_count.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetSecurityCountCmd. Asserts the security universe size is exactly 26885 for the SH market snapshot. With the response body being a fixed 2-byte count, this fixture pins down the exact wire-format value so any change to the TDX universe size is flagged by the test.

nodes.name: security_list.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetSecurityListCmd. Captures num_records=1000 (one page of the SH universe) and the first record (code 999999 / 上证指数, pre_close 3966.171142578125). Used to assert that list parsing returns a full page, that code/name GBK decoding works, and that `pre_close` is a TDX-decimal float (verified to ~1e-2 precision).

nodes.name: security_quotes.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetSecurityQuotesCmd. One-record snapshot (code 600000, pre_close 9.93) with unknown_2=-1 and unknown_3=22694 captured alongside. Documents server-side fields whose meaning has not yet been reverse-engineered; pair file for `security_quotes.hex`.
nodes.languageNotes: unknown_2 / unknown_3 fields track the project's TDX-protocol reverse-engineering ledger; tests assert these are preserved rather than swallowed by the decoder.

nodes.name: transaction.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetTransactionDataCmd. Captures num_records=10 plus the first tick (14:59, price 9.9, vol 0 — the vol=0 row reflects a closing-call record). Pair file for `transaction.hex`. Used to validate intraday tick decoding, decimal price, and the 0x122f wire format.

nodes.name: xdxr_info.json
nodes.summary: Ground-truth fixture for GetXdxrInfoCmd. Records num_records=87 dividend/split/ex-rights events plus the first entry (1999-11-10, category 5). Pair file for `xdxr_info.hex`. The `category=5` value maps through `_normalize_per_10_shares` to verify per-10-share scaling rules are applied correctly.

nodes.name: Claude Settings (Local Permissions)
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code permissions manifest (87 lines) that grants allow-list access to many Bash, MCP, and Skill patterns the maintainer uses while developing easy-tdx. Entries cluster around: repo-local git operations (add/commit/push/stash/tag/diff/rm/ls-remote), Python tooling (python/python3/uv/pip install/pip uninstall/pip show/pip list/pip index/mypy, ruff check/format), all 20+ easy-tdx CLI subcommands (`easy-tdx ping/version/quote/kline/tick/transaction/auction/quote-list/board-list/board-members/belong-board/capital-flow/unusual/market-stat/server-info/symbol-info/ex`), GitHub CLI (gh run/auth/repo/api), PyPI publishing helpers (twine upload, curl pypi.org), and MCP tools (zread, web-reader, context7, web-search-prime, compound-engineering, episodic-memory, playwright, zai-mcp). Notable one-off allow-rules snapshot Windows paths from the maintainer's earlier `xmtdx` prototype and embed a heredoc Bash rule that auto-runs ruff check --fix + ruff format on any *.py file the Edit tool touches. Contains no project logic — only a permissions allow-list, so never checked into PyPI or shipped to end users.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON with a single `permissions.allow` array of strings. Most entries follow the pattern `Bash(<command-prefix> *)` or `Bash(<exact-prefix> *)`. Several entries embed escaping artifacts from a Windows path string (e.g. `D:\\python\\xmtdx\\...`) — these are developer-machine-specific allow-rules and harmless outside that machine. MCP tools are referenced by the `mcp__<server>__<tool>` naming convention Claude Code uses internally.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: .understandignore file (54 lines) for the in-repo /understand pipeline; mirrors the .gitignore-style exclude syntax (globs, comments, !-negation, trailing-/ directory markers). Every line is currently commented out — the file documents the suggested exclusion set rather than enforcing it. Built-in defaults listed in the header (`node_modules/`, `.git/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `obj/`, `*.lock`, `*.min.js`) are always excluded unless negated. The body groups suggestions by category: from .gitignore (`*.py[cod]`, `.eggs/`, `.mypy_cache/`, `.ruff_cache/`, `.pytest_cache/`, `.omc/`, `.claude/`, `.playwright-mcp/`, `docs/_build/`, `FK/`, `.coverage`, `signals.json`, `src/easy_tdx/exchange_margin.py`, `CLAUDE.md`, `audit-report-*.html`); detected directories (`docs/`, `examples/`, `scripts/`, `tests/`); and language-specific test-file patterns (JS/TS `*.test.*`, C# `**/*Tests.cs`, Java `**/*Test.java`, Go `**/*_test.go`). Because everything is commented, the file currently acts as a template showing future maintainers what the analyzer should skip on demand.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain text file following .gitignore syntax (one pattern per line, `#` for comments, leading `!` for negation). Uses section-header comments to group suggestions (e.g. `# --- From .gitignore (uncomment to exclude) ---`). All 40+ content lines are commented out, so the runtime behavior is exactly the built-in defaults.

nodes.name: Sphinx Config (conf.py)
nodes.summary: Sphinx documentation configuration for the easy-tdx docs site (41 lines). Sets project='easy-tdx', copyright='2025, Justin Gu', author='Justin Gu', and pulls the release string from `importlib.metadata.version('easy-tdx')` via the `_get_version` helper so the docs always match the installed package version — single source of truth, no manually-bumped version constant. Enables the `myst_parser` extension with `colon_fence` and `deflist` for Markdown-style directive/definition-list rendering, suppresses `myst.xref_missing` cross-reference warnings so relative `.md`/LICENSE links don't break the build, and sets `html_theme = 'sphinx_rtd_theme'` with `navigation_depth=3`. `source_suffix` accepts both `.rst` and `.md` so authors can write either format.
nodes.languageNotes: Python module — Sphinx loads it as `conf.py`. Imports `importlib.metadata` lazily inside `_get_version()` rather than at module top so the package metadata doesn't have to be importable at conf-load time (matters for editable installs). Uses MyST extensions (`colon_fence` for fenced code blocks, `deflist` for definition lists) and supports both reStructuredText and Markdown source suffixes.

nodes.name: _get_version
nodes.summary: Helper that returns the installed easy-tdx version string via `importlib.metadata.version('easy-tdx')`. Called once at conf-load time and assigned to Sphinx's `release` global so doc pages reflect the actual shipped version instead of a hand-maintained literal.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-line function; does an inline import (`from importlib.metadata import version`) inside the body so the lookup happens only when needed.

nodes.name: PyInstaller Windows EXE Spec
nodes.summary: PyInstaller spec file (114 lines) that bundles easy-tdx into a single Windows GUI EXE (`dist/easy-tdx.exe`). Anchors on `src/easy_tdx/__main__.py`, uses `--onefile` + `console=False` (no console window — Windows GUI subsystem) + `upx=True` for size compression, and pulls in hidden imports for `uvicorn` (uses importlib to load protocol implementations dynamically, so PyInstaller's static analysis misses them), `easy_tdx` (routers/backtest.strategies are lazily imported so full-module collection is needed), and `pystray` (system-tray icons use win32 API + Pillow image-format plugins both loaded via importlib). Data files collected: `tzdata` (IANA timezone DB) and all of `PIL` (Pillow's image format plugins). Critically, the spec globs `web-ui/dist/**/*` and registers every individual file as a `(src_path, 'web_dist/...')` tuple — a v1.19.1 bug where `datas=[('web-ui/dist', 'web_dist')]` silently failed (treated the directory as a missing file) is documented inline to prevent regression; the correct fix is to enumerate the files. Excludes `matplotlib`, `tkinter`, `PyQt5/6`, `PySide2/6`, `pytest`, `IPython`, `jupyter`, `notebook` from the bundle.
nodes.languageNotes: Python DSL PyInstaller reads (`Analysis`/`PYZ`/`EXE` constructors). Uses `collect_submodules` from `PyInstaller.utils.hooks` and a manual `Path('web-ui/dist').rglob('*')` glob loop to translate a directory into per-file tuples (the v1.19.1 trap). The `if _dist_root.is_dir()` guard falls back to a printed `WARNING` instead of failing the build so CI can still produce an EXE when frontend assets haven't been built yet (UI is then blank at runtime).

nodes.name: async_connect.py
nodes.summary: Example script (67 lines) demonstrating the async TdxClient API. Docstring lists the 1:1 method parity with sync TdxClient (`get_security_count`, `get_security_list`, `get_security_bars`, `get_security_quotes`, …) and explains AsyncTdxClient-specific quirks: each instance owns one TCP connection, concurrent calls serialize via an internal `asyncio.Lock`, `async with` closes both the connection and the heartbeat task, and the heartbeat interval is 60 s (vs 15 s for sync). `main()` opens two clients — one pinned to `180.153.18.170` (manual host) and one via `AsyncTdxClient.from_best_host()` — runs them in sequence, and reports the SH security count plus 5 daily K-lines for 600000 浦发银行. The trailing `# 运行结果` block shows a sample OHLCV output (2026-05-18 → 2026-05-22) so users can sanity-check their first run.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level `async def main()` driven by `asyncio.run(main())`. Uses async-context-manager `async with` and `await` on each `get_*` call. Imports `from easy_tdx import AsyncTdxClient, KlineCategory, Market` at top-level — the only example in the batch that exercises the async client.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async entrypoint that opens two sequential AsyncTdxClient sessions (one manually pinned to `180.153.18.170`, one via `from_best_host()`), calls `get_security_count(Market.SH)` and `get_security_bars(Market.SH, '600000', KlineCategory.DAY, 0, 5)`, and prints the SH count plus the 5-bar OHLCV string.
nodes.languageNotes: `async def`; structured as two `async with` blocks run in sequence within the single event loop created by `asyncio.run(main())`.

nodes.name: connect_best_host.py
nodes.summary: Example (38 lines) that demonstrates the three connection modes of the sync `TdxClient`. The docstring walks through each one: (1) `TdxClient()` reads `best_host` from `~/.easy_tdx/config.json` and is the recommended daily-use entry point after running `from_best_host()` at least once; (2) `TdxClient.from_best_host()` concurrently pings all candidate servers, picks the lowest-latency one, and persists the choice to `config.json`; (3) `TdxClient(host)` connects directly to a manually-specified IP. All three modes support `with` context-manager syntax that closes the connection and heartbeat thread on exit. The script exercises modes 1 and 2 in two sequential `with` blocks, printing `(host, port)` and the SH security count for each.
nodes.languageNotes: Three sequential `with TdxClient... as c:` blocks executed at module import time (no `if __name__ == '__main__'` guard). Accesses private attribute `c._host` / `c._port` for diagnostic printing only.

nodes.name: ping_servers.py
nodes.summary: Example (51 lines) showing how to measure latency to every 通达信 server without ever establishing a TdxClient. `TdxClient.ping_all()` is a static method — no connection object needed — that concurrently TCP-pings the candidate list (default `KNOWN_HOSTS`, ~50+ IPs) and returns a `list[tuple[str, float]]` sorted by ascending latency. The script wraps the result in a pandas DataFrame, multiplies the seconds column by 1000 to get milliseconds, and prints the table. Trailing 20-row `# 运行结果` block lists the 20 lowest-latency servers at the time the example was captured, anchored on `115.238.56.198` at 12.35 ms.
nodes.languageNotes: Single top-level statement chain (no functions). Uses `pandas.DataFrame(results, columns=["服务器", "延迟(s)"])` for display, then `df["延迟(ms)"] = df["延迟(s)"] * 1000`. Imports only `pandas` and the static method on `TdxClient`.

nodes.name: market_stat.py
nodes.summary: Example (38 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_market_stat()`, which reads three 通达信 builtin indices (880005 全市场行情统计, 880001 总市值指数, 880006 涨跌停统计) and returns a one-row DataFrame with up/down/neutral counts, suspended count (residual = total − up − down − neutral, not a protocol-defined field), total_amount, total_volume, total_market_cap (price × 1e10), and limit_up/limit_down counts. Prints the transposed DataFrame to fit a wide table on one screen. Trailing `# 运行结果` shows a sample (5420 stocks total, 68 涨停 / 12 跌停).
nodes.languageNotes: Single `with TdxClient.from_best_host() as c:` block. Uses `info.T.to_string(index=False)` to print a single-row wide DataFrame vertically.

nodes.name: security_count.py
nodes.summary: Minimal example (30 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_security_count(market)`. Connects via `from_best_host()`, calls the method for `Market.SH` and `Market.SZ`, prints both counts. Docstring defines the `Market` enum (`SZ=0`, `SH=1`, `BJ=2`) and warns that `Market.BJ` (北京证券交易所) results may be unstable on some servers — discouraged for production use. Returned count includes stocks, funds, bonds, and indices (i.e. all security types).
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level straight-line script: one `with` block, two `c.get_security_count(...)` calls, two `print()` calls. Sample output shows 沪市=2847, 深市=3612.

nodes.name: security_list_all.py
nodes.summary: Largest market-info example (148 lines). Demonstrates `TdxClient.get_security_list_all()` which fetches all 沪深 A 股 (SH + SZ, BJ excluded) with 通达信行业 + 申万行业 columns populated from the server-managed `tdxhy.cfg` (~1MB config file). The docstring walks through the four cost drivers: download tdxhy.cfg, query both markets for security counts, paginate (1000/page), filter to A-share prefixes (沪 60/68, 深 00/30), then map industries. Caching layer: `pages='all'` (default) caches results to `~/.easy_tdx/cache/security_list_all.json` for 24h; pass `pages=N` to skip caching. The script enables `logging.INFO` to surface per-page progress, bumps `timeout=30.0` so full-page requests don't time out, calls `get_security_list_all()` once, then prints a self-documenting schema table (8 fields, English/Chinese/type/description) followed by the first 20 rows of A-share. Trailing `# 运行结果` confirms 5318 沪深 A 股 across 7 pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Single `with` block. Uses `pandas.DataFrame.to_string(index=False)` for both the schema-printout and the head-20 printout. Imports `logging` and `pandas` alongside `TdxClient`.

nodes.name: security_list.py
nodes.summary: Example (124 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_security_list(market, start)` — the paginated single-market variant. Each call returns one page of ~1000 records (one market, all security types); the `start` arg controls the page offset. Industry columns (`industry_tdx`, `industry_sw`) are returned as empty strings here — the docstring points users to `get_security_list_all()` for industries. Script mirrors the schema pattern used in `security_list_all.py`: prints an 8-field legend (market/code/name/volunit/decimal_point/pre_close/industry_tdx/industry_sw) followed by `head(20)` of the SH page 0 result. Trailing sample shows 浦发银行, 白云机场, 东风汽车 etc.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirror of security_list_all.py structure but with `c.get_security_list(Market.SH, start=0)` instead of `get_security_list_all()`. Single call, single `head(20)` printout.

nodes.name: security_quotes.py
nodes.summary: Example (66 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_security_quotes(stocks)` — batched real-time five-level quotes for up to 80 stocks per request. Docstring documents the `SecurityQuote` schema: 基础信息 (market, code, server_time), prices (price, pre_close, open, high, low), 量额 (vol, cur_vol, amount, s_vol, b_vol), 五档 (bid1–bid5/bid_vol1–5 + ask1–ask5/ask_vol1–5), and price metrics (rise_speed, limit_up, limit_down — limit fields are `None` and need `get_price_limits()` to populate). The script fetches four A-share blue chips (600000 浦发银行, 600519 贵州茅台, 000001 平安银行, 000858 五粮液), derives `change_pct` in-line, and prints a curated projection (code/price/change_pct/open/high/low/pre_close/vol/amount).
nodes.languageNotes: Builds `stocks = [(Market.SH, "600000"), ...]` list, passes it in one call. Adds a derived column `df["change_pct"] = (df["price"] - df["pre_close"]) / df["pre_close"] * 100` before the print. Limit-up/down fields are not populated by this call.

nodes.name: index_bars.py
nodes.summary: Example (60 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_index_bars(market, code, category, start, count)` for OHLCV bars on the major Chinese indices (上证 999999/000001, 深证成指 399001, 创业板指 399006, 上证50 000016, 沪深300 000300, 中证500 000905, 中证1000 000852). The docstring notes: vol/amount on indices represent the entire covered-market aggregate (not just the index's tickers); prices are in 'points', not CNY. Script pulls 10 daily bars for 上证指数 (999999, SH) and prints the DataFrame. Sample output spans 2026-05-11 → 2026-05-22 with prices around 3370 点.
nodes.languageNotes: Single call: `c.get_index_bars(Market.SH, "999999", KlineCategory.DAY, 0, 10)`. Imports `KlineCategory` alongside `Market` and `TdxClient`.

nodes.name: security_bars.py
nodes.summary: Most detailed K-line example in the batch (85 lines) for `TdxClient.get_security_bars(market, code, category, start, count)`. Docstring enumerates all 11 `KlineCategory` values (MIN_1/5/15/30/60, DAY, WEEK, MONTH, YEAR, SEASON, YEAR_ALT — note the gap on value 8), explains the daily-vs-minute DataFrame shape swap (`date` for daily-plus, `datetime` for minute bars), and documents the `bar_time='start'|'end'` parameter: `start` (default) keeps 通达信's convention where a 5-min bar at 11:25 marks the start (the protocol returns the bar-start moment); `end` shifts to the right edge (11:30) to align with Tushare / 同花顺 / 聚宽. Script pulls daily K-line for 江特电机(002176) plus two parallel 5-min calls (start vs end) and prints both for visual comparison.
nodes.languageNotes: Three sequential calls in one `with` block: one daily `get_security_bars(SZ, "002176", DAY, 0, 10)` and two 5-min calls differing only in the `bar_time=` kwarg. Imports `KlineCategory`, `Market`, `TdxClient`.

nodes.name: history_minute_data.py
nodes.summary: Example (47 lines) for `TdxClient.get_history_minute_time_data(market, code, date)` — fetches intraday 1-minute bars for a single historical date. Docstring: `date` is an int in YYYYMMDD format (e.g. 20250110); the returned DataFrame has 240 rows for a full A-share 4-hour trading day (09:30–11:29 + 13:00–14:59), columns are `datetime` ('HH:MM:SS'), `price` (CNY, float), `vol` (shares, int). Non-trading days return an empty DataFrame. Script fetches 浦发银行 (600000) for 2025-01-10 and prints `head(20)`. Sample output shows prices in the 10.24–10.26 CNY range with thousands of shares per bar.
nodes.languageNotes: Single call: `c.get_history_minute_time_data(Market.SH, "600000", 20250110)`. Module-level execution, no function definitions.

nodes.name: minute_time_data.py
nodes.summary: Example (46 lines) for `TdxClient.get_minute_time_data(market, code)` — today's intraday 1-minute bars (≈240 rows). Identical DataFrame schema to `get_history_minute_time_data` (datetime/price/vol), but the docstring notes: pre-open bars have price=0 and vol=0; non-trading-hours calls return an empty DataFrame. Script fetches 浦发银行 (600000) on whatever the current day is and prints head(20). Sample output captured at 2025-01-10 shows same first-20 bars as the history variant — confirms API shape parity.
nodes.languageNotes: Twin of history_minute_data.py structurally — only the method name and date arg differ.

nodes.name: history_transaction.py
nodes.summary: Example (50 lines) for `TdxClient.get_history_transaction_data(market, code, date, start, count)` — historical intraday tick-by-tick distribution for a specific YYYYMMDD date. Schema (datetime 'HH:MM:SS', price CNY, vol shares, num merged-tick count, buyorsell 0=买 / 1=卖 / 2=中性撮合 / 8=集合竞价). The script maps `buyorsell` to a Chinese directional label and prints the first 20 ticks of 浦发银行(600000) on 2025-01-10 14:56. Sample output shows a mix of directional trades and 集合竞价 closing prints at 14:59.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `df["buyorsell"].map({0: "买", 1: "卖", 2: "中性", 8: "集合竞价"})` to add a 方向 column before printing a 4-column projection (`datetime`/`price`/`vol`/`方向`). Note protocol precision is only 1 minute, not sub-second.

nodes.name: transaction_data.py
nodes.summary: Example (49 lines) for `TdxClient.get_transaction_data(market, code, start, count)` — today's intraday ticks (paged via `start` offset, default count 800). Schema identical to history_transaction (datetime/price/vol/num/buyorsell). Same `方向` column mapping pattern. Sample output is identical to `history_transaction.py` (both pulled during the 2025-01-10 14:56 → 14:59 window) — confirms the today's-tick API and the historical API produce identical record shapes for the live session.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirror of history_transaction.py with `c.get_transaction_data(...)` instead of `get_history_transaction_data(...)`. No `date` argument.

nodes.name: company_cli.sh
nodes.summary: Bash recipe sheet (69 lines) for the three `easy-tdx` finance-info commands: `finance-info`, `company-info` (no-arg form = F10 category list, with-arg form = F10 section body), and the JSON / CSV output-format switches. Sets up `CODE=600519` (贵州茅台) and `MARKET=SH`, then walks through: (1) finance-info with `--table` (default tabular output); (2) bare company-info to list categories; (3) four named-section reads (公司概况, 股本结构 default 1024 bytes, 分红扩股 with `--length 4096` for long bodies, 龙虎榜单); (4) format switches (`--output json` for finance-info, `--output csv` for category listing); (5) advanced direct-file read using `${CODE}.txt` + `--offset 0 --length 2048`; (6) error-handling demo with a non-existent section to show 'available sections' prompting. All command lines are commented out — the file is a cookbook to copy snippets from, not an executable.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash script with `echo` separators and `# easy-tdx ...` lines that are commented. Designed to be read and adapted, not run directly. Uses `================================================================` and `--- N.M title ---` section dividers.

nodes.name: company_info.py
nodes.summary: Example (103 lines) walking through the two-step F10 company-info flow: (1) `TdxClient.get_company_info_category(market, code)` returns a category DataFrame (columns: name, filename, start, length), and (2) `TdxClient.get_company_info_content(market, code, filename, start, length)` reads the GBK-encoded body for a chosen category. Docstring enumerates the 16 standard F10 categories (最新提示, 公司概况, 财务分析, 股本结构, 股东研究, 资本运作, 业内点评, 行业分析, 公司大事, 研究报告, 经营分析, 主力追踪, 分红扩股, 高层治理, 龙虎榜单, 关联个股). Defines `show_category_content(client, categories, category_name, max_chars=500)` helper that filters the category DataFrame by name, fetches the section, truncates to `max_chars` with a '共 X 字' suffix, and prints it. Loop iterates all `SHOW_CATEGORIES` so the user sees every section's first 500 chars.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a top-level `def show_category_content(...)` plus a module-level `with TdxClient.from_best_host() as c:` block. Uses `categories[categories["name"] == category_name].iloc[0]` to locate the entry. Constants `CODE=600519`, `NAME='贵州茅台'`, `MARKET=Market.SH`.

nodes.name: show_category_content
nodes.summary: Helper that looks up a category by name in the F10 category DataFrame and prints its content (truncated to `max_chars`). Early-returns with a '未找到分类' message when the name is absent. Strips the body and appends a '共 N 字' footer when truncated so the user sees total length.
nodes.languageNotes: Signature: `show_category_content(client, categories, category_name, max_chars=500)`. Uses `row.iloc[0]` to extract the single matching row, then `client.get_company_info_content(MARKET, CODE, r['filename'], int(r['start']), int(r['length']))` to fetch the body.

nodes.name: company_web_api.py
nodes.summary: Web-API consumer example (134 lines) that calls easy-tdx's REST endpoints (`GET /api/v1/finance`, `GET /api/v1/company/category`, `GET /api/v1/company/content`) using `requests`. Mirrors the F10 contract from `company_info.py` but split into two endpoints (list-then-fetch) so programs can manage `filename`/`offset`/`length` explicitly. Defines four helpers — `fetch_finance()`, `fetch_company_category()`, `fetch_company_content(filename, offset, length)`, `find_section(categories, section_name)` — and the `__main__` guard drives through three scenarios: (1) finance for 贵州茅台, (2) category listing, (3) read 「公司概况」 (first 1024 bytes) and 「股本结构」 (full length).
nodes.languageNotes: Modern `from __future__ import annotations` + type hints; `dict[str, ...]` return types. Module-level constants `BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1'`, `MARKET = 'SH'`, `CODE = '600519'`. Uses `resp.raise_for_status()` for error propagation and `timeout=15` on every call.

nodes.name: fetch_finance
nodes.summary: GET /finance helper that returns the JSON-decoded body (`{"data": [{...financial fields...}]}`) for the most recent single-period 37-field financial snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Single `requests.get(...)` + `raise_for_status()` + `return resp.json()`. Annotations: `-> dict`.

nodes.name: fetch_company_category
nodes.summary: GET /company/category helper returning `{"data": [{"name":..., "filename":..., "start":..., "length":...}, ...]}` — the F10 category index needed before reading any section body.
nodes.languageNotes: Identical pattern to fetch_finance but targets the category endpoint.

nodes.name: fetch_company_content
nodes.summary: GET /company/content helper reading one F10 section's GBK text body. Required args: `filename` (from the category index), `offset` (default 0), `length` (default 1024). Returns the `content` substring of the JSON response.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds the params dict explicitly so missing offset/length default to 0/1024. Returns `resp.json()["content"]` — only the body substring.

nodes.name: find_section
nodes.summary: Helper that linear-scans a category-index JSON for a section whose `name` equals `section_name`. Returns the matching row dict (`{name, filename, start, length}`) or `None` when not found.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns `dict | None` (modern union syntax with `from __future__ import annotations`). Iterates `categories.get("data", [])` so a missing key returns `None` cleanly.

nodes.name: finance_info.py
nodes.summary: Example (97 lines) for `TdxClient.get_finance_info(market, code)` — returns a single-row DataFrame with ~30 financial fields (股本类 万股, 资产负债类 元, 利润类 元, 每股指标) in pinyin-abbreviated column names. Docstring groups the schema by category (capital structure: liutong_guben, zong_guben, guojia_gu, faren_gu, b_gu, h_gu, faqiren_faren_gu, zhigong_gu; basics: province, industry, updated_date, ipo_date, gudong_renshu; balance sheet: zong_zichan, liudong_zichan, guding_zichan, wuxing_zichan, liudong_fuzhai, changqi_fuzhai, ziben_gongjijin, jing_zichan; P&L: zhuying_shouru, zhuying_lirun, yingshou_zhangkuan, yingye_lirun, touzi_shouyu, jingying_xianjinliu, zong_xianjinliu, cunhuo, lirun_zonghe, shuihou_lirun, jing_lirun, weifen_lirun; per-share: meigujing_zichan). Script fetches 贵州茅台 and prints the DataFrame transposed (`info.T`) so the wide single-row layout renders vertically.
nodes.languageNotes: Single call: `c.get_finance_info(Market.SH, "600519")`. Uses `info.T.to_string(header=False)` to print one column per line.

nodes.name: price_limits.py
nodes.summary: Example (38 lines) for `TdxClient.get_price_limits(market, code, name, pre_close)` — computes the (limit_up, limit_down) pair based on the stock's board. Docstring tabulates the rules: 普通 A 股 ±10%, ST/*ST ±5%, 科创板 (688) ±20%, 创业板 (300/301) ±20%, 北交所 (43/83/87/92) ±30%. Edge cases: 上市首日 / 科创板 & 创业板前 5 个交易日 unlimited (returns `(None, None)`); indices/板块 codes unlimited; results rounded to 2 decimals. Script fetches the live quote for 600519 to get pre_close, calls `get_price_limits`, prints the three values.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-call sequence: `c.get_security_quotes([(Market.SH, CODE)])` to read pre_close, then `c.get_price_limits(Market.SH, CODE, NAME, q["pre_close"])`. Sample output for 贵州茅台: pre_close=1498.00 → limit_up=1647.80, limit_down=1348.20.

nodes.name: xdxr_info.py
nodes.summary: Example (58 lines) for `TdxClient.get_xdxr_info(market, code)` — full ex-rights / ex-dividend history. Returns an `XdxrRecord` DataFrame (typically dozens of rows for an A-share). Schema: date (YYYY-MM-DD), market, code, category (1–14), name, plus per-event fields fenhong, peigujia, songzhuangu, peigu, suogu, xingquanjia, fenshu, panqian_liutong, panhou_liutong, qian_zongguben, hou_zongguben. Docstring maps the 14 event types (1=除权除息, 2=送配股上市, 3=非流通股上市, 4=未知股本变动, 5=股本变化, 6=增发新股, 7=股份回购, 8=增发新股上市, 9=转配股上市, 10=可转债上市, 11=扩缩股, 12=非流通股缩股, 13=送认购权证, 14=送认沽权证), the 前复权 formula, and notes that `easy-tdx` auto-converts the protocol's '每10股' values into '每股' units (e.g. 19.26 is per-share dividend, not 192.6). Script fetches 贵州茅台 and prints the last 10 events (`tail(10)`); sample shows 10 consecutive 除权除息 (category=1) entries from 2021-06 to 2025-09.
nodes.languageNotes: Single call returning a wide DataFrame; prints `tail(10).to_string(index=False)` with comment '仅显示 fenhong/peigujia/songzhuangu/peigu 四个核心除权字段' — the other columns are populated but hidden in the demo to keep the output compact.

nodes.name: Finance & F10 README
nodes.summary: README (135 lines) for the 06_finance examples directory. Documents the 通达信-origin (TdxClient) finance / company-info commands as distinct from the 新浪三表 `f10` HTTP fetcher elsewhere in the project. Tabulates the three commands and their Web API counterparts: `finance-info` / `GET /api/v1/finance` (single-period 37-field snapshot), `company-info` (no-arg) / `GET /api/v1/company/category` (F10 category index), `company-info "板块名"` / `GET /api/v1/company/content` (F10 section body). Explains the two-mode `company-info` (no name = list categories; name = read section, auto-loops over the category's `length` so even huge sections finish in one call). Lists the 16 standard F10 categories with one-line Chinese descriptions, then cross-references the example files: `finance_info.py`, `company_info.py`, `company_cli.sh`, `company_web_api.py`. Ends with three quick-start blocks (CLI / Python / Web API) and a finance-info / company-info column-by-column field reference.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with GFM tables (declared via `| col | col |` syntax), three fenced code blocks (bash / python / bash), and a multi-section TOC structure (`## company-info 命令的两种用法`, `## F10 板块完整列表`, `## 示例文件`, `## 快速开始`, `## 字段说明`). Uses inline backticks for command names and field identifiers.

nodes.name: block_info.py
nodes.summary: Example (52 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_block_info(filename)`, which downloads+parses one of the three 通达信 system-block files (`block_zs.dat` 行业, `block_gn.dat` 概念, `block_fg.dat` 风格) and returns a `TdxBlock` DataFrame with columns name/category/count/codes. Docstring defines the three block files by size and classification (zs ~80 行业, gn ~500+ 概念, fg ~50 风格) and notes that codes are 6-digit strings without market prefix. Script calls `get_block_info('block_gn.dat')` via `TdxClient.from_best_host()` and prints the first 20 concept-board rows (含H股, 含B股, 基金重仓, 跨境支付, 区块链, 智能穿戴, etc.).
nodes.languageNotes: Single `with TdxClient.from_best_host() as c:` block; uses `c.get_block_info(...)` followed by `df[["name", "category", "count"]].head(20).to_string(index=False)`. Imports `TdxClient` from the package root.

nodes.name: fund_flow.py
nodes.summary: Example (58 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_fund_flow(market, code)` which returns a single-row DataFrame (`FundFlow` model) with the four-level order-flow breakdown (超大单/大单/中单/小单 in/out, all in CNY). Docstring defines the four order-amount thresholds (超大 >100万 / 大 20-100万 / 中 4-20万 / 小 ≤4万) plus the two derived metrics (主力净流入 = (super+large in) - (super+large out), 全单净流入 = total in - total out). Script pulls the live fund-flow for 600519 (贵州茅台), converts in/out columns from CNY to 亿元 (divide by 1e8), and prints the in/out/净流入 grid.
nodes.languageNotes: Single `with` block. Builds a derived DataFrame from `flow[col].iloc[0] / 1e8` for visualization then adds the `净流入(亿) = 流入 - 流出` column. Imports `Market`, `TdxClient` from the package root.

nodes.name: history_fund_flow.py
nodes.summary: Example (50 lines) demonstrating `TdxClient.get_history_fund_flow(market, code, start, count)` for multi-day order-flow history (HistoricalFundFlow DataFrame). Docstring notes the implementation detail: the implementation first tries Category 22 (direct historical-fund-flow category); on empty servers response it transparently falls back to recomputing from daily K-line + tick data (slower path). Schema is `date` + the same 8 order-flow columns as `fund_flow.py`. Script fetches 10 days for 600519 and prints the full table.
nodes.languageNotes: Single `with` block calling `c.get_history_fund_flow(Market.SH, '600519', 0, 10)`. Imports `Market`, `TdxClient` from the package root.

nodes.name: block_fg.dat
nodes.summary: Pre-downloaded block_fg.dat snapshot (~453 KB on disk) of the 通达信 系统风格板块 (style boards, ~50 boards classified by 市值/估值/地域). Binary 通达信 custom format starting with the ASCII marker `Registry ver:1.0 (1999-9-28)` followed by 0-padding, then per-block records (board-name UTF-8 + member codes). Written to disk by `report_file.py`'s loop over `AVAILABLE_FILES`; the file proves the 7709 KNOWN_HOSTS pipeline can deliver the style-board file successfully without zipping or signing.

nodes.name: block_gn.dat
nodes.summary: Pre-downloaded block_gn.dat snapshot (~757 KB on disk) of the 通达信 系统概念板块 (concept boards, 500+ boards classified by current-market themes). Largest of the three block files; same binary header (`Registry ver:1.0 (1999-9-28)`) and per-block record layout as block_fg.dat/block_zs.dat. Cached output of `report_file.py`; provides concrete local evidence that the 概念 boards (582 in the live demo) are reachable through the standard KNOWN_HOSTS download endpoint.

nodes.name: block_zs.dat
nodes.summary: Pre-downloaded block_zs.dat snapshot (~330 KB on disk) of the 通达信 系统行业板块 (industry boards, ~82 boards following the 申万 industry classification: 房地产, 电力行业, 计算机设备, 电子元件, 通信服务, ...). Same binary header and record layout as the other two block files. Cached output of `report_file.py`; the docstring + sample output show 82 boards (vs ~80 mentioned in the docstring of block_info.py — minor version-of-server drift is expected).

nodes.name: gpcw20260331.dat
nodes.summary: Unzipped historical financial-record snapshot (~12.4 MB, 35219 lines reported by wc) covering report period 20260331 (Q1 2026). Binary 通达信 gpcw format: per-stock fixed-width records with 6-digit ASCII code strings (000001, 000002, 000004, 000006, ...) prefixed by a small header packet. The companion zip `gpcw20260331.zip` (~5 MB on disk) sits next to it in the parent directory. Parsed by `read_history_financial()` into 5342 `FinancialRecord` rows; each record has market/code/report_date plus ~280 float fields covering 每股指标 / 资产负债表 / 利润表 / 现金流量表. Pairs with the `examples/10_offline/history_financial.py` example.
nodes.languageNotes: Binary file (data). 4-byte little-endian header + per-record variable payload. Stores 6-digit stock codes as ASCII strings terminated by \x00; report_date is encoded in the header (0100 eb25 35 01 → 0x0135_25eb with mask).

nodes.name: tdxhy.cfg
nodes.summary: Pre-downloaded industry-mapping snapshot (143,523 bytes / 5613 lines reported by wc — note: `wc -l` counts 0-terminator-free sections and \r-style breaks). ASCII text file mapping stock code → 通达信 industry code (e.g. `0|000001|T1001|||X500102`, `0|000002|T110201|||X530101`). Header columns: 0=market (0=SZ, 1=SH), then code, then T-prefix 通达信 industry code, then 申万 industry code and region (X-prefix). Downloaded from the 7709 KNOWN_HOSTS endpoint via `get_report_file('tdxhy.cfg')` and consumed by `get_security_list_all()` to fill `industry_tdx` / `industry_sw` columns on the cross-market A-share list.

nodes.name: report_file.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive example (178 lines) covering all four file-download surfaces of TdxClient: get_report_file (KNOWN_HOSTS / 行情服务器 / 7709: tdxhy.cfg + block_*.dat), get_financial_file_list / get_financial_file / get_financial_records (CALC_HOSTS / 计算服务器 / 7727: historical financial zip + parsed FinancialRecord DataFrame). Docstring tabulates which files live on which server (KNOWN_HOSTS provides tdxhy.cfg + block_*.dat; CALC_HOSTS provides tdxfin/gpcw*.zip; the legacy KNOWN_HOSTS files `base_info.zip`, `gpcw.txt` now return empty packets and are documented as broken). Script (1) probes the broken legacy files to confirm empty response, (2) downloads AVAILABLE_FILES (tdxhy.cfg + 3 block files) and saves them to `OUTPUT_DIR`, (3) parses `block_zs.dat` for inspection, (4) opens a second TdxClient session against `CALC_HOSTS[0]` to fetch file list, pick the first non-trivial file, download the zip, save it, and parse records.
nodes.languageNotes: Two sequential `with TdxClient(...) as c:` sessions: first against `from_best_host()` (行情服务器, 7709) for block files, then against `CALC_HOSTS[0]` (计算服务器, 7727) for financial files. Uses `OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / 'downloads'` so all bytes land next to the script. Imports `CALC_HOSTS`, `TdxClient` from the package root.

nodes.name: block_data.py
nodes.summary: Example (139 lines) demonstrating the offline block-data API trio: `detect_tdx_home()`, `read_block_dat(path)`, `read_customer_blocks(dir)`. Docstring covers both system blocks (TdxBlock dataclass with name/category/count/codes, stored at `vipdoc/block_zs.dat|block_gn.dat|block_fg.dat`) and user blocks (CustomerBlock with blockname/block_type/codes, stored at `T0002/blocknew/blocknew.cfg + TDXBlockN.blk`). Script implements lookup priority: try local file first, fall back to online via `TdxClient.from_best_host()` only for boards that aren't on disk; the local-vs-online fallback is the architectural pattern that distinguishes 10_offline/ from the always-online TdxClient examples. Defines a small `_print_blocks` helper that prints the first 5 codes per board + an ellipsis.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines `def _print_blocks(blocks: list[TdxBlock], title: str)` at module level. Uses `Path(home) / 'vipdoc'` and `Path(home) / 'T0002' / 'blocknew'` as the standard on-disk locations. Imports `TdxBlock` from `easy_tdx.models.finance`; `detect_tdx_home`, `read_block_dat`, `read_customer_blocks` from `easy_tdx.offline`.

nodes.name: _print_blocks
nodes.summary: Module-level helper that prints up to 5 boards from a `list[TdxBlock]` (name + 成分股数 + first 5 codes + ellipsis for longer lists). Used twice in the script: once for each system-block read, and once for the user-block listing.
nodes.languageNotes: Signature: `_print_blocks(blocks: list[TdxBlock], title: str) -> None`. Iterates `blocks[:5]` and composes the `', '.join(codes[:5])` line.

nodes.name: daily_bars.py
nodes.summary: Example (83 lines) demonstrating offline `.day` daily-bar reading. Docstring documents both lookup modes (1: `Market` + `code` via `find_daily_bar_file`, 2: explicit path), the file layout (`vipdoc/{sh,sz}/lday/{exchange}{code}.day` e.g. `vipdoc/sh/lday/sh600000.day`), the SecurityBar dataclass (open/close/high/low/vol/amount + year/month/day + hour=0/minute=0 for daily bars), and the four magic price-coefficient multipliers (A stock price×0.01 + vol×0.01; SH/SZ indices price×0.01 + vol×1.0; funds price×0.001 + vol×1.0 or ×0.01; bonds price×0.001 + vol×1.0). Script reads 浦发银行 600000 daily bars via `find_daily_bar_file(Market.SH, '600000')` then prints the last 10 trading days as a date/open/high/low/close/vol grid.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports from `easy_tdx` (Market) and `easy_tdx.offline` (detect_tdx_home, find_daily_bar_file, read_daily_bars). Pattern: `home = detect_tdx_home()` → early `raise SystemExit(1)` if None → `bars = read_daily_bars(filepath)` → print last 10.

nodes.name: detect_home.py
nodes.summary: Example (157 lines) demonstrating the offline path-detection suite: `detect_tdx_home()`, `resolve_vipdoc()`, and the four `find_*_bar_file` locators (daily / 5-min / 1-min / 5-min lc). Docstring tabulates the entire `vipdoc/` directory layout: per-market `sh/`, `sz/`, `ds/` (扩展) subdirectories + per-bar-type subdirectories (`lday/`, `fzline/`), the binary record layouts (`.day` for daily, `.5` for legacy 5-min OHLC×100, `.lc1`/`.lc5` for new-minute OHLC-float), and the `T0002/` paths (hq_cache/gbbq, blocknew/, fin/). Also documents the detection priority (TDX_HOME env > Windows-guess `C:\new_jyplug`, Linux/macOS guess `~/new_jyplug`). Script runs through four scenarios — auto-detect, manual `resolve_vipdoc` walk, code-based file resolution for 4 instrument/bar combinations, and env-var setup instructions for all three platforms.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports `Market` from `easy_tdx` and five offline functions (`detect_tdx_home`, `find_5min_bar_file`, `find_daily_bar_file`, `find_lc1_bar_file`, `resolve_vipdoc`) from `easy_tdx.offline`. Uses `vipdoc.rglob('*')` for directory counts and a list of `(label, lambda)` tuples for locator demos. Sample output shows `home=C:\new_jyplug` and per-file `存在/不存在` status.

nodes.name: ex_daily_bars.py
nodes.summary: Example (89 lines) demonstrating extended-market daily bars (期货/港股/外盘指数/宏观经济) read from `vipdoc/ds/lday/{market_code}#{code}.day` (e.g. `29#A1801.day` for futures, `12#A_IXIC.day` for NASDAQ). Docstring documents the ExDailyBar dataclass (open/high/low/close as IEEE-754 float — no price coefficient; amount/vol integers; settlement for futures; hk_stock_amount for HK-specific reinterpretation) and the 32-byte-per-record layout (date 4B + OHLC 4×4B float + amount 4B + vol 4B + settlement 4B float). Script lists the first 10 `.day` files in the ds directory, picks the first one, and prints its last 5 bars with the closing-date / OHLC / 结算价 grid.
