nodes.name: test_multi_tick_charts_oversized_minutes_no_crash
nodes.summary: Issue #10 回归：minutes ≥ 1440（如累计/异常值）不应抛 ValueError。  用户报告 hour must be in 0..23, not 1039（即 minutes // 60 == 1039）。 修复后应按 % 24 折算为日内时刻，保证不崩溃且结果合理。

nodes.name: TestFK
nodes.summary: FK 趋势指标（布尔输出）。  慢线用 SLOPE(CLOSE,21)*20 做斜率外推：上涨时慢线被正斜率推高， 下跌时被负斜率压低。FK = fast(EMA2) > slow(外推 EMA42)， 语义是"价格是否突破趋势外推线"，本质是动量/反转偏离检测： - 强下跌时 fast 相对外推慢线偏高 → FK=True（超卖/反弹信号） - 强上涨时慢线被推高，fast 难以超越 → FK=False（未超买或接近超买）

nodes.name: test_task_runner_submit_and_poll
nodes.summary: 提交任务后应能轮询到 done 状态与结果。

nodes.name: test_task_runner_captures_failure
nodes.summary: 任务内异常应记入 error，状态变 failed。

nodes.name: test_task_runner_lru_eviction
nodes.summary: 超过上限应丢弃最旧的非 running 任务。  注意：淘汰发生在 submit 时，淘汰对象是「当时最旧的非 running 任务」。 用 max_workers=1 串行执行时，哪个任务被淘汰取决于提交速度 vs 执行速度 的竞态（快机器上 t0 还在 running 会被跳过，慢机器上 t0 已完成会被淘汰）。 所以本测试不断言「特定 task_id 被淘汰」，而是验证： (1) 存活的 non-running 任务数 ≤ max_results (2) 最后提交的任务一定存活（它是最近的，不可能被 LRU 淘汰） (3) 至少有 2 个任务被淘汰（5 提交 - 3 上限 = 2）

nodes.name: test_task_runner_unknown_task_raises
nodes.summary: 查询不存在的 task_id 应抛 KeyError。

nodes.name: test_task_runner_does_not_evict_running
nodes.summary: LRU 淘汰应跳过 running 任务——这是审计修复的关键并发正确性回归。  若回退此修复，正在执行的长任务会被新提交的任务淘汰，导致 worker 在 move_to_end 时抛 KeyError、任务结果丢失。

nodes.name: test_task_runner_shutdown_is_idempotent_and_rejects_submit
nodes.summary: shutdown 应幂等，关闭后再 submit 应报错。

nodes.name: test_task_runner_worker_tolerates_eviction
nodes.summary: worker 即使在运行期间被（理论上的）淘汰也不应抛未捕获异常。  直接验证 _run 的 move_to_end 容忍 KeyError：手动从 _tasks 移除条目后 触发完成路径。

nodes.name: test_param_rejects_int_from_nan
nodes.summary: int(nan) 应报 ValueError（→400）而非 ValueError 逃逸。

edges.reason: release.yml's header comment explicitly notes that the EXE release runs in parallel with PyPI publishing to maximize release resilience: both share the `v*` tag trigger and a single failing workflow does not block the other.
ci.yml runs `pip install -e .[dev]` and `pip install -e .[dev,web]` which materialize the editable install declared in pyproject.toml (including the hatchling force-include of web-ui/dist).
ci.yml runs `pip install -r requirements-dev.txt` after the editable install, so the workflow is bound to whatever is pinned there.
`python -m build` consumes pyproject.toml (hatchling backend) to produce sdist + wheel; the hatchling force-include brings web-ui/dist into the wheel.
`pip install -e .[web,packaging]` pulls fastapi/uvicorn + pystray/Pillow extras declared in pyproject.toml; the workflow depends on the exact extras list.
release.yml header comment notes that EXE release runs in parallel with PyPI publish to maximize release resilience — they share the same `v*` tag trigger but no actual data dependency.

`.readthedocs.yaml` declares `sphinx.configuration: docs/conf.py`, so the RTD build driver reads the project's Sphinx config (theme extensions, MyST setup) from there. This is the canonical production-docs entry point.

README's installation section (`pip install easy-tdx`, `pip install -e .[dev]`, `pip install -e .[web]`) directly references the optional-dependency extras declared in pyproject.toml — the README is the user-facing docs mirror of the [project.optional-dependencies] table.

README mentions the dev install path (`pip install -e .[dev]`) but does not enumerate the pinned versions — the actual locked toolchain (pytest 9.0.3, mypy 2.1.0, ruff 0.11.11, scipy 1.15.3) lives in requirements-dev.txt. README points developers at pyproject's [dev] extra; the lock file is the reproducible ground truth.

README's `## 更新日志` footer section points at CHANGELOG.md; the CHANGELOG in turn lists every feature/README change per release (e.g. v1.19.1 README rewrite, v1.18.0 strategy-library additions). They are paired narrative + changelog docs.

README's `CLI 命令汇总` section enumerates every click command (ping, version, kline, quote, quote-list, tick, transaction, board-*, capital-flow, auction, unusual, market-stat, server-info, symbol-info, announcement, f10, finance-info, company-info, indicator, indicator-list, chanlun, offline, serve). All of these are wired through `easy_tdx.cli:cli` (the click group declared as the console-script entry point in pyproject.toml).

README's `完整 API 列表` table for TdxClient/AsyncTdxClient (get_security_count, get_security_list, get_security_quotes, get_security_bars, get_index_bars, get_minute_time_data, get_history_minute_time_data, get_transaction_data, get_history_transaction_data, get_fund_flow, get_history_fund_flow, get_xdxr_info, get_finance_info, get_company_info_category, get_company_info_content, get_block_info, get_report_file, get_market_stat, get_price_limits) maps 1:1 to the methods on TdxClient/AsyncTdxClient in client.py.

README's `统一客户端` section describes `UnifiedTdxClient` with examples calling `client.get_stock_kline(Market.SH, "600519", Period.DAILY, count=5)` and `client.goods_kline(ExMarket.HK_MAIN_BOARD, "00700", Period.DAILY, count=5)` — the exact public surface of `easy_tdx.unified.UnifiedTdxClient`.

README's `MacClient / AsyncMacClient` API table (get_stock_quotes, get_stock_quotes_list, get_stock_kline, get_stock_kline_with_indicators, get_tick_chart, get_tick_charts, get_chart_sampling, get_transactions, get_symbol_info, get_board_list, get_board_members, get_board_summary, get_board_ranking, get_board_change_ranking, get_belong_board, get_capital_flow, get_auction, get_unusual, get_server_info, get_kline_offset, get_goods_list) corresponds to the public methods on `easy_tdx.mac.client.MacClient/AsyncMacClient`.

README's `MacExClient / AsyncMacExClient` API table (goods_count, goods_list, goods_quotes, goods_quotes_list, goods_kline, goods_tick_chart, goods_chart_sampling, goods_transaction, plus goods_transaction_all for HK stock markets) maps directly to `easy_tdx.ex.mac_client.MacExClient/AsyncMacExClient`. The README is the canonical reference for the HK-stock-specific goods_transaction_all semantics (倒序 start=0, 1800/page, 9万条 safety cap).

README's `缠论分析` section (CLI examples `easy-tdx chanlun SZ 000001 --table`, multi-level section, output examples with 笔数/中枢数/线段数/买卖点/背驰) and Python section (`from easy_tdx.chanlun import ChanlunAnalyser, ChanlunConfig`) document the public surface of the chanlun subpackage.

README's `回测引擎` section introduces the `Strategy` base class and shows `from easy_tdx.backtest import Strategy` + `from easy_tdx import MyTT`. The advanced `量化因子与组合管理` section imports `FactorEngine`, `FactorAnalyzer`, `RebalanceEngine`, `FactorWeightedOptimizer`, plus slippage models (`SquareRootSlippage`) and execution models (`TWAPExecution`) — all public re-exports of the backtest subpackage.

README's `离线数据读取` + `离线数据写入同步` sections import from `easy_tdx.offline` (detect_tdx_home, read_daily_bars, find_daily_bar_file, encode_daily_bar, append_daily_bars, get_last_bar_date, encode_5min_bar, append_5min_bars, encode_lc_min_bar, append_lc_min_bars, sync_daily_bars_from_security_bars) — these are the documented public API of the offline subsystem.

README's `公告检索（巨潮资讯网）` section describes `from easy_tdx.cninfo import CninfoClient` + `client.get_announcements(...)` + `client.download_pdf(...)`. Documents the 巨潮资讯网 independent data source (no TDX connection required).

README's `财报三表（新浪财经）` section documents `from easy_tdx.sina import SinaClient` + `client.get_financial_report(code, report_type='lrb'/'fzb'/'llb')`. Documents the 新浪财经 independent data source for three financial statements.

README's `Web API` section documents `easy-tdx serve` startup, `http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs` Swagger UI, `/redoc` ReDoc, port + reload flags, `_resolve_web_dist_dir()` three-tier detection, and the FastAPI mount path. All of these are behaviors implemented in `easy_tdx.web.app`.

README's `技术指标` section enumerates the 34 supported indicators (MACD, KDJ, RSI, BOLL, DMI, ATR, WR, CCI, BIAS, BIAS_SIGNAL, OBV, VR, EMV, MFI, BRAR, ASI, TRIX, DPO, MTM, ROC, EXPMA, BBI, PSY, DFMA, CR, KTN, XSII, MASS, TAQ, ZHUOYAO, SAR, VWAP, AROON, FK) — the canonical list implemented in `easy_tdx.indicator`.
README's backtest example imports `from easy_tdx import MyTT` and uses `MyTT.MA(close, 10)` — documents the 麦语言 indicator library as the user-facing API surface.

README's architecture diagram lists `src/easy_tdx/config.py` as the module owning `服务器地址、端口、超时配置`. The server-settings UI section (v1.19.7) documents the user-visible behavior of `get_best_host`/`save_best_host`/`get_best_mac_host`/`save_best_mac_host` (host pollution guard).

CHANGELOG v1.19.1 (referenced in README's release-highlight narrative) documents `python -m easy_tdx` as the PyInstaller entry point; the `__main__` module makes `serve` the default subcommand when no args are passed.

README's top blurb about 'strategy library (SQLite persistence)' + 'pick multiple strategies for capital-split combo backtest' documents the StrategiesView UI page (kind: 'multi' save, load rerun-to-today, 单标的/组合 tab).

README's introduction highlights the 'free + one-line install' value prop and CHANGELOG v1.19.7 (called out in README blurb) adds the server settings UI for picking TDX hosts. ServerSettingsView.vue is the page that lists 50+ hosts and lets users ping/switch.

README's top-of-page S/A/B/C/D grading blurb documents the public grading API (`gradeBacktest` / `gradePortfolio` / `gradeOptimizePoint` in `web-ui/src/grading/index.ts`) and the 6-dimension weighted + 一票否决 semantics (calmar 18% / max_drawdown 17% / win_rate 17% / profit_factor 18% / sharpe 15% / volatility 15%).

README's `自带策略示例` table lists 15 named strategies (双均线交叉/EMA12-EMA50/MACD金叉/布林带突破/RSI反转/KDJ金叉/海龟/乖离率反转/量价/捉妖大师/DMI/CCI/MFI/TRIX/MTM/OBV); CHANGELOG v1.17.0 expanded the builtin count to 18 (added 唐安奇, 肯特纳, ATR通道, WR, BIAS, EMV, DPO, BBI). All of these are classes in `easy_tdx.backtest.strategies.builtin`.

CHANGELOG v1.20.0 headline (自动 ping 切换) and detailed entries describe `_reconnect.py` as the single-point injection of `_execute` into 8 clients (TdxClient/MacClient/ExTdxClient/MacExClient, sync+async), the cross-host failover (`select_best_host_sync/async`), the empty-data failover (`find_working_host_sync/async`), and the unified `_reconnect`/`_areconnect` helper. The changelog entry is the authoritative spec for this module.

CHANGELOG v1.20.0 names `client.py` as the home of `_reconnect`/`_areconnect` unified-rebuild helpers and the v1.19.7 `AsyncTdxClient.reconnect_to(host)` hot-switch method; v1.19.4 credits `client.py` for `best_host` cache-pollution guard; v1.16.2 credits client.py for several audit fixes (audit #1 atomic-write, #2 retry delays, #5 fail-fast bar_time, #10 closure-binding, #18 aware datetime). The changelog is dense with client.py callouts.

CHANGELOG v1.19.4 explicitly names `src/easy_tdx/config.py` as the module that added the `best_mac_host` field + `get_best_mac_host()` / `save_best_mac_host()` and the `get_best_host` cache pollution guard (3 regression tests in `tests/unit/test_config.py::TestBestHostPollutionGuard`). v1.16.2 audit #18 cites config.py for naive-datetime→aware migration.

CHANGELOG v1.19.4 (MAC host no longer pollutes standard best_host — explicit `src/easy_tdx/mac/client.py` citation); v1.16.2 audit #12 (honest docstring on `asyncio.gather` being serial on a single TCP connection); v1.17.2 QFQ negative-price fix in `MacClient.get_stock_kline`.

CHANGELOG v1.17.2 names `mac/adjust.py` as a new pure-function module (`compute_forward_factor` / `apply_forward_adjust` / `has_bad_prices`) with no easy_tdx internal dependencies. Documents the QFQ local-recompute formula: `f = (P_cum - fenhong + peigujia×peigu) / (P_cum×(1+songzhuangu+peigu))`.

CHANGELOG v1.19.1 names `src/easy_tdx/web/app.py` for `_resolve_web_dist_dir()` three-tier detection (env var → _MEIPASS/web_dist → repo root web-ui/dist) and `app.mount('/', StaticFiles(..., html=True))`. v1.19.2 (MIME types via `mimetypes.add_type`), v1.19.3 (SPAStaticFiles subclass for Vue Router fallback on /optimize /portfolio etc.), v1.19.5 (4th-tier pkg-internal easy_tdx/web/dist fallback).

CHANGELOG v1.19.7 explicitly names `src/easy_tdx/web/routers/server.py` for the 3 server-settings endpoints (`GET /api/v1/server/hosts`, `POST /api/v1/server/test`, `POST /api/v1/server/switch`). Documents the host-validation rule (only IPs in the candidate list allowed).

CHANGELOG v1.17.4 names `web/routers/backtest.py` as the home of 6 backtest endpoints plus the v1.17.4 `POST /backtest/optimize-all/run/async` 7th endpoint (process-pool fan-out across 18 strategies with `_optimize_one_strategy` top-level function for pickling); v1.17.11 `POST /backtest/multi-strategy/run/async`; v1.18.1 `workers` field.

CHANGELOG v1.18.0 expands `SavedStrategyCreate.kind` Literal from `['single', 'portfolio']` to `['single', 'portfolio', 'multi']`. v1.18.1 fixes a ruff E501 line-length violation on the same field. Documents the schema-evolution contract.

CHANGELOG v1.17.11 explicitly names `src/easy_tdx/web/strategy_store.py` as the SQLite single-file CRUD store at `~/.easy_tdx/strategies.db`, threaded-safe (`check_same_thread=False` + serial write lock), honoring `EASY_TDX_CONFIG_DIR` env override (same convention as config.py).

CHANGELOG v1.17.0 introduces the background task runner (ThreadPoolExecutor + LRU task table, status-aware eviction, lifespan-shutdown hook); v1.17.0 audit fixes name `task_runner.py` for the LRU-races-running bug fix and get_runner double-checked locking. v1.17.8 increases a flaky CI timeout in `tests/unit/test_web_backtest.py::test_task_runner_does_not_evict_running`.

CHANGELOG v1.17.0 explicitly expands the builtin strategy count from 5 to 18, enumerating the 13 new ones by category: 趋势类 (EMA双线交叉, 三均线, DMI, TRIX), 通道/突破类 (唐安奇, 肯特纳, ATR通道), 震荡/反转类 (CCI, WR, BIAS, EMV, DPO), 均线类 (BBI). v1.18.1 audit code-style fix in registry.py.

CHANGELOG v1.17.13 names `src/easy_tdx/backtest/multi_strategy_engine.py::_build_combined_equity` as the site of the `drawdown / initial` → `drawdown / peak` bug fix (with `peak_safe = peak.where(peak != 0, 1.0)` for divide-by-zero). Documents why the fix was needed (545% gain caused 5× drawdown inflation).

CHANGELOG v1.17.9 enumerates 4 fixes in `backtest/performance.py::compute`: (1) `avg_win/avg_loss/max_win/max_loss` switched from absolute P&L to per-trade return (`pnl / cost_basis`); (2) `_compute_avg_holding_days` parses real `datetime.date` instead of YYYYMMDD int subtraction (cross-month inflation); (3) `profit_factor=0.0` when no losses → `999.0`; (4) `np.isfinite` requires `to_numpy(dtype=np.float64)` on object-dtype columns.

CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #4 cites `backtest/execution.py` (and `backtest/orders.py`) for the stop-loss fix: stop orders now fill at next bar's open with gap protection (SELL takes `min(open, trigger_price)`). Documents the execution-model extension points (TWAP/VWAP/Limit).

CHANGELOG v1.17.0 introduces `ParamGridOptimizer` (itertools.product, 200-grid cap, skips param-range validation during optimization). v1.17.4 documents the `_optimize_one_strategy` extraction needed for process-pool pickling.

CHANGELOG v1.17.4 introduces `backtest/strategies/presets.py` as the standalone preset-grid file: 18 strategies × 1-2 key params × reasonable value lists, single source of truth consumed by both the optimize page auto-fill and the one-click-optimize-all endpoint. Single-strategy cartesian product ≤ 200.

CHANGELOG v1.17.0 introduces the strategy registry (Param schema + ParametrizedStrategy base + @register_strategy); v1.17.0 audit #13 adds NaN/Inf/giant-int rejection in `Param.validate`; v1.18.1 fixes a ruff E501 line-length violation.

CHANGELOG v1.17.5 cites `src/easy_tdx/ex/mac_client.py` (sync + async `goods_transaction`) for the HK-stock protocol routing fix (issue #14): 港股 stock markets (HK_INDEX/HK_MAIN_BOARD/HK_GEM/HK_FUND/HK_STOCK_GGT/HK_DARK_POOL) now route to ex extension protocol (0x23FC 当日 / 0x2406 历史) instead of MAC 0x122F (which returned 39-byte empty body for HK). v1.17.6 adds `goods_transaction_all` automatic pagination.

CHANGELOG v1.17.5 introduces `src/easy_tdx/ex/_hk_transaction.py` for the HK-stock extension-protocol implementation (`_fetch_all_hk_transactions_sync/async`, `ExTransactionRecord → MacTransaction` mapping with price ÷ 1000 for HKD float). v1.17.6 expands it with the 1800/page full-day pagination (50-page safety cap = 90000 records).

CHANGELOG v1.17.2 cites `mac/commands/symbol_bar.py` for the QFQ negative-price fix (root cause: TDX MAC server returns raw negative prices for long-term heavy-adjustment stocks at deep history offsets, e.g. 601088 2013-11-18 QFQ close=-3.80).

CHANGELOG v1.16.1 (issue #10) names `mac/commands/tick_charts.py` for the multi-day tick chart fix: `time(minutes // 60, minutes % 60)` → `time(minutes // 60 % 24, minutes % 60)` — `% 24` guard against abnormally large `minutes` values (user saw 62340).

CHANGELOG v1.15.4 (issue #8) names `commands/security_quotes.py` for the ETF/指数/基金/可转债/国债 3-decimal price parsing fix (was 2-decimal hardcoded `/100.0`, now `divisor = 10 ** 位数` based on `_price_decimal_digits(market, code)`).

CHANGELOG v1.18.3 cites `src/easy_tdx/commands/security_bars.py` for K-line truncated-body tolerance: discards the tail-record if the byte-stream ends mid-record, returns the successfully-parsed prefix. v1.19.3 removes the `if bars:` guard so empty-body cases return `[]` instead of raising 500.

CHANGELOG v1.19.1 names `src/easy_tdx/cli/cmd_web.py` for the `--open-browser` / `--no-open-browser` flags (`threading.Timer(1.5, ...)` after uvicorn start, disabled under `--reload` to avoid focus theft in dev).
CHANGELOG v1.19.1 introduces `src/easy_tdx/tray.py` for the system-tray icon (pystray-based K-line icon, right-click 'open browser / quit'). Required for the no-black-window EXE packaging target.

CHANGELOG v1.17.9 names 3 regression guards added to `tests/unit/test_backtest_performance.py`: `test_avg_holding_days_crosses_month_boundary` (must use real calendar days, not YYYYMMDD int diff), `test_profit_factor_no_losing_trades_is_large` (== 999.0), `test_avg_win_zero_when_no_cost_basis_column` (safe degradation).

CHANGELOG v1.19.4 cites `tests/unit/test_config.py::TestBestHostPollutionGuard` (3 tests: MAC host auto-reset / valid host untouched / reset persistence) + `TestMacHostSeparation` (2 tests: save_best_mac_host doesn't touch best_host / get_best_mac_host returns independent field). Documents the regression test surface for the v1.19.4 host-pollution fix.

CHANGELOG v1.17.5 cites `tests/unit/test_hk_transaction.py` (new file, 22 tests) + `tests/fixtures/ex_history_transaction.hex` and `.json` (real recorded 00700 response from 2026-07-03) for the HK-stock protocol routing regression suite.

CHANGELOG v1.17.2 introduces `tests/unit/test_mac_qfq_adjust.py` (16 tests for `compute_forward_factor` / `apply_forward_adjust` / `has_bad_prices` covering pure cash dividends, songzhuangu, multi-event, invalid factor skipping, input immutability, latest-price anchoring) + `tests/unit/test_mac_qfq_integration.py` (4 tests with monkeypatched MacClient).

CHANGELOG v1.17.15 introduces `web-ui/src/data/investment-quotes.ts` (100 quotes from Buffett/Munger/Graham/Lynch/Soros/Livermore/Taleb/Dalio, Chinese) and `web-ui/src/components/QuoteCarousel.vue` (Fisher-Yates shuffle, 3000ms interval, orange progress bar, fade/slide 350ms transition).

CHANGELOG v1.17.10 cites `web-ui/src/views/OptimizeView.vue::buildBacktestQuery` for the URL query with `symbol/startDate/endDate/category`. v1.18.1 documents the workers selector. v1.18.3 documents the 8-process default + localStorage persistence (`optimize.workers`). v1.17.15 documents the orange run-all button styling.

CHANGELOG v1.17.10 cites `web-ui/src/views/BacktestView.vue` for the `onMounted` route.query backfill (symbol/startDate/endDate/category); v1.17.0 cites it for initial parameter rendering; v1.18.1 cites it for the market-prefix unification via `detectMarket`.

CHANGELOG v1.17.4 introduces `web-ui/src/market.ts::detectMarket` for the 6-digit-code-based market inference (北交所 43/83/87/92/920/93 + 4xx/8xx, 沪市 6xx/9xx/5xx, else 深市). 17 boundary cases (贵州茅台/宁德时代/北交所各段/ETF) all pass.

CHANGELOG v1.17.11 introduces `web-ui/src/views/StrategiesView.vue` (route /strategies, card grid, save/load/delete, multi-strategy combo backtest). v1.18.0 expands it with `kind: 'multi'` save, `↻ 重跑到今天`, single/portfolio tab split, holding 3-state highlighting, overfit warning. v1.18.1 adds combination grading via `gradePortfolio`.

CHANGELOG v1.17.14 introduces the grading UI: `GradeBadge.vue` (round badge S-gold/A-green/B-blue/C-orange/D-red) + `GradeDetails.vue` (expandable dimension bars + disqualification reason + sample-size warning). v1.17.14 self-tests in `web-ui/src/grading/__tests__/grade.test.ts` (15 tests).

CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #1 names `offline/write_daily.py` for the atomic-write fix (`fsync + flush` + post-write `_repair_tail` + read-side `get_last_bar_date` integrity check). Out-of-scope-of-audit enhancement: command-query separation enforced on get-functions.

CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #16 cites `offline/paths.py` for path-traversal rejection (sanitize `/`, `\`, `..` from user-supplied codes to keep them inside vipdoc; preserve `#` which is common in 通达信 filenames).
CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #15 (mtime-cache in parallel scanner path) and audit #6 (per-symbol exception logging + ≥50% failure-rate summary alert) both cite `screen/scanner.py`.

CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #5 cites `_df.py` (and 4 client files) for the `bar_time` fail-fast fix (invalid values now raise `ValueError` instead of silently using the default). v1.16.0 names `_df.py` for the `bar_time='start'/'end'` switch and the shared `_apply_bar_time_align_df` / `_apply_bar_time_align_bars` helpers reused across all 3 K-line paths.
CHANGELOG v1.16.2 audit #14 cites `unified.py` for adding `DeprecationWarning` on duplicate methods between `UnifiedTdxClient` and its sub-clients (rather than hard-removing, for backward compat).

CHANGELOG v1.17.9 cites `engine.py::_compute_pnls` for filling the new `cost_basis` field on SELL trades (moving-weighted-average cost × sell quantity). v1.17.2 also names engine in the context of backtest crashes on negative QFQ prices.

CHANGELOG v1.17.4 cites `backtest/orders.py` for removing `this_close`/`worst`/`best` trade-price modes (kept only `next_open`/`next_close` to avoid look-ahead bias). v1.16.2 audit #4 also cites orders.py for the stop-loss gap protection.

welcome-discussion's quickstart (`pip install easy-tdx` + 3 sample commands) duplicates the README's `## 安装` + `## CLI 参考` intros. Explicit README link at the end ('完整文档看 README'). They share the user-onboarding narrative.

welcome-discussion's 3 quickstart commands (`easy-tdx kline SZ 000001 --count 30 --table`, `easy-tdx indicator MACD -m SH -c 600519 --table`, `easy-tdx serve`) map directly to the click commands registered in easy_tdx.cli.

welcome-discussion mentions `easy-tdx serve` + browser opens `http://localhost:8000/docs` for the FastAPI Swagger UI — same flow documented in the README's `Web API` section and implemented by `easy_tdx.web.app`.

`[project.scripts] easy-tdx = "easy_tdx.cli:cli"` declares the console-script entry point: the CLI binary `easy-tdx` invokes the `cli` symbol exported from `src/easy_tdx/cli/__init__.py`. This is the contract between package metadata and CLI bootstrap.

`name = "easy-tdx"` + `version = "1.20.0"` + `description = "通达信 TCP 协议行情数据客户端..."` populate the installed package's `__version__` / `__doc__` / pkg metadata, read by `easy_tdx.__init__._get_version` and by the CLI `version` subcommand.

Loose coupling: pyproject.toml declares the package, Read the Docs builds docs/ which often references package-level APIs. No direct import; both are project-root artifacts read by external tooling (pip / RTD).

`[project.optional-dependencies].dev` declares `pytest/mypy/ruff/scipy` with lower-bound-only pins; `requirements-dev.txt` then provides exact-version pins for the same toolchain. They are the upper/lower halves of the dev-dependency contract.

Reverse direction: the locked toolchain (pytest 9.0.3, mypy 2.1.0, ruff 0.11.11, scipy 1.15.3) is the de-facto enforcement of the `[dev]` extra. CI's `pip install -r requirements-dev.txt` after `pip install -e .[dev]` ensures the wheel's lower bounds are supplemented with the exact locked versions.

edges.description: The backtest engine plan precedes the factor engine plan chronologically (2026-06-09 then 2026-06-12) and establishes the BacktestResult/trade types that later factor-based pipelines ultimately consume.
v1.11.0 delivers the Factor ABC, registry, and FactorEngine; v1.12.0 directly builds on top by adding preprocessing and FactorAnalyzer for IC/quantile/turnover/decay evaluation.
v1.12.0 factor analysis funnels factor scores into v1.13.0, where RebalanceEngine consumes FactorEngine.compute_cross_section outputs to drive multi-period portfolio backtests.
v1.13.0 closes the factor-to-portfolio loop; v1.14.0 then hardens the realism of those backtests with pluggable slippage and execution models while remaining backward compatible.
v1.14.0 introduces the slippage and execution models whose cost components are decomposed in v1.15.0 by AttributionAnalyzer's cost_attribution method.

edges.reason: Document section 6 is the authoritative design for `BacktestEngine` (cTor signature with `cash=100000, commission=0.0003, min_commission=5.0, stamp_tax=0.001, slippage=0.0, execution='next_open', position_mode='full', order_reject_policy='reduce', benchmark=...`, plus `run(df, chanlun_result=None) -> BacktestResult`) and the four-step pipeline (signal → simulator → portfolio → performance). This is P0 implementation.

Section 4 fully specifies the `Strategy` ABC (`init()`, `next()`, `I(func)`, `buy/sell`, `data` proxy, `position`, `chanlun`), `StrategyDataProxy` (`__getattr__` exposes arbitrary DataFrame columns), and `_SeriesAccessor` (`[0]` current, `[-1]` previous). Documented example `MACrossStrategy(MaCrossStrategy)` matches the implementation's `MaCrossStrategy` in `strategies/builtin.py`.

Section 3 defines the four core dataclasses `Signal`/`Trade`/`Position`/`BacktestResult` (including `BacktestResult.config['future_leak_warning']` flag and the `to_json`/`to_dict`/`summary` methods). Implemented verbatim in `types.py`.

Sections 6.3-6.6 enumerate the five execution rules (`next_open`/`next_close`/`this_close` with future-leak warning/`worst`/`best`), four position modes (`full`/`fixed`/`percent`/`signal_only`), two order-reject policies (`reduce`/`skip`), and commission/StampTax/slippage formulas — all of which drive the `OrderSimulator` implementation. Future-leak warning contract is enforced per the spec.

Pipeline step 3 implements `PortfolioTracker` (cash + position + market value + drawdown tracking) which produces the `equity_curve` columns that the spec promises in `BacktestResult.equity_curve` (`datetime, cash, position_value, total, drawdown, drawdown_pct`).

Section 7 table is the full definition of the 18 metrics in `PerformanceAnalyzer` (total/annual return, max_drawdown + duration, sharpe/sortino/calmar, win/lose trades, rejected_trades, win_rate, profit_factor, avg_win/loss, max_win/loss, avg_holding_days, volatility, alpha, information_ratio). Spec keys (`sharpe`, `sortino`, `calmar`, etc.) must match the implementation output exactly.

Section 5 fully specifies the P1 `@dsl_strategy` decorator + string-compiler path (Python-function-call capture → `(buy_mask, sell_mask)` recording → dynamic `Strategy` subclass generation) and the built-in DSL function table reusing `MyTT.py` (`MA`/`EMA`/`RSI`/`BOLL`/`MACD`/`CROSS`/`REF`/`HHV`/`LLV`/`BETWEEN`/`COUNT`). `dsl.py` implements this — non-P0 but explicitly deferred, not omitted.

Section 8 documents the `easy-tdx backtest` CLI surface: `--strategy` / `--strategy-file` / `--params` / `--grid` / `--indicators` / `--table` / `--output csv` plus auto-fetching K-lines. `cli.py` provides the `backtest` Click command with `_load_strategy` / `_load_strategy_from_file` / `_load_combo_strategies`.

Section 4.3 example `MACrossStrategy` (init registers MA5/MA20 via `self.I(MA, ...)`, then emits buy/sell on `crossover()`) maps directly to the `MaCrossStrategy` in `strategies/builtin.py`. The spec lists 18 indicator strategies as reference patterns (`MaCross`, `Macd`, `BollBreakout`, etc., all visible in `exportsByPath`).

Section 3 specifies the new `SlippageModel` ABC (`compute(price, size, volume, volatility, direction) -> float`) plus four implementations (`FixedSlippage`/`PercentSlippage`/`SquareRootSlippage`/`VolumeSlippage`). Section 3.3 documents the A-share σ × √(Q/V) formula and degradation rule. `slippage.py` implements exactly this.

Section 4 specifies the `ExecutionModel` ABC and four implementations (`ImmediateExecution` for backward compat, `TWAPExecution(n_bars=5)` with 100-share-lot rounding, `VWAPExecution(n_bars=5, volume_lookback=20)` by historical volume weights, `LimitExecution(ttl_bars=5)` with low/high target checks). `execution.py` implements the ABC + the four models.

Section 5 specifies `AttributionAnalyzer` (constructor takes `trades` + `equity_curve` + optional `benchmark`/`factor_exposures`/`factor_returns`/`groups`) and three decomposition methods (`brinson_attribution` using Brinson-Hood-Beebower Total = Allocation + Selection + Interaction; `factor_attribution` using R = Σ(β_i × f_i) + α; `cost_attribution` breaking down commission/slippage/stamp_tax) plus the `AttributionReport` dataclass. `attribution.py` implements both the analyzer and report.

Section 4.6 explicitly documents the change to `engine._generate_signals`: when `execution_model` is set, the loop changes from `for signal in signals: trades = simulator.simulate([signal], ...)` to `for signal in signals: sub_trades = execution_model.execute(...); all_trades.extend(sub_trades)`. Sections 3.4 and 4.6 add the `slippage_model=` and `execution_model=` kwargs to `BacktestEngine.__init__`. When both `slippage_model` and `slippage` are passed, `slippage_model` wins.

Section 3.4 states `OrderSimulator` gains a `slippage_model: SlippageModel | None = None` parameter; when non-None it bypasses the legacy `self.slippage` scalar and calls `slippage_model.compute()`. Section 6 explicitly preserves `OrderSimulator(df, slippage=0.01)` for backward compatibility.

Section 2 file-table marks `types.py` as modified with 'new AttributionReport' added in v1.15.0; v1.14.0 has no types changes. The current `types.py` (per batches.json) does not yet export `AttributionReport`, confirming this future change.

Spec header explicitly states '前置: 方案 A（v1.11.0–v1.13.0）已完成' — these plan-B enhancements build on the backtest engine approved in `doc:spec-backtest-engine` (rev 2). Section 6 commits to full backward compatibility for `BacktestEngine(strategy, slippage=0.01)` callers.

Section 3.2 documents a future extension where `BacktestEngine` gains a `factors=` kwarg and `FactorEngine` output is auto-injected into `StrategyDataProxy`. This bridges the planned Plan B (advanced backtest) back to factor signals — currently deferred.

Section 9 lists the test file `test_backtest_strategy.py` covering Strategy base + indicator injection + precomputed columns + chanlun injection — coordinated with the CI coverage gate (`--cov src/easy_tdx --cov-fail-under=60`). The spec explicitly states all tests are offline with hand-rolled DataFrame fixtures.
Section 9 lists `test_backtest_engine.py` for the core engine pipeline (signal → matching → portfolio). Combined coverage of all 7 listed test files (~50 tests) drives the CI ≥60% threshold.

Section 9 lists 8 test files for factor + portfolio (`test_factor_base.py`/`test_factor_engine.py`/`test_factor_builtin.py`/`test_factor_transform.py`/`test_factor_analysis.py` + three `test_portfolio_*`); tests are 100% offline (no network) using `np.random`-generated price fixtures.

test_commands_offline.py (line 343) loads history_minute_time hex and asserts a 240-bar full-session decode with first-bar price=10.29, vol=10044 as encoded in this manifest.

test_commands_offline.py (line 314) loads minute_time hex and asserts GetMinuteTimeCmd returns 240 current-day bars, with the first bar carrying the price=0.01/vol=48/_unknown_1=54 tuple encoded in this manifest.

test_commands_offline.py (lines 105, 136, 155) loads security_bars hex three times to assert GetSecurityBarsCmd parses OHLCV records correctly across partial/full/boundary slicing — the first-record OHLCV (10.25/10.25/10.08/10.12/41151820.0) is the oracle for all three cases.
test_commands_offline.py::test_security_count_parse (line 29) loads security_count hex and asserts the 2-byte decoder yields the integer 26885 encoded in this manifest.

test_commands_offline.py (lines 48, 90) loads security_list hex and asserts GetSecurityListCmd yields 1000 records with first record code=999999/name=上证指数/pre_close=3966.171142578125 exactly as recorded here.

test_commands_offline.py::test_security_quotes_parse (line 175) loads security_quotes hex and asserts GetSecurityQuotesCmd surfaces code=600000/pre_close=9.93 while tolerating the unknown_2/unknown_3 slots documented in this manifest.

test_commands_offline.py (line 365) loads transaction hex and asserts GetTransactionCmd returns 10 ticks with first tick at 14:59, price=9.9, vol=0 — the zero-volume sentinel here doubles as a VWAP/divide-by-vol regression check.
test_commands_offline.py (line 423) loads xdxr_info hex and asserts GetXdxrInfoCmd decodes 87 ex-rights/ex-dividend events with first event 1999-11-10 category=5 (拆股) as encoded in this manifest.
test_decode_errors.py (line 39) reuses the xdxr_info hex for error-path coverage, so this manifest documents the baseline (87 records, category=5) the error-path test compares against.
test_commands_offline.py instantiates GetSecurityCountCmd(Market.SH) and calls parse_response, so this test is the offline oracle for src/easy_tdx/commands/security_count.py.

The PyInstaller Analysis entry-point is `src/easy_tdx/__main__.py`; pathex=['src'] ensures the package is importable during the static analysis pass.
`hiddenimports += collect_submodules('easy_tdx')` forces PyInstaller to bundle every submodule under src/easy_tdx/, including routers / backtest.strategies that are imported lazily.

The spec's `hiddenimports += collect_submodules('pystray')`, `collect_data_files('tzdata')`, and `collect_data_files('PIL')` rely on those packages being installed via `pip install -e .[web,packaging]` (declared in pyproject.toml) before `pyinstaller easy_tdx.spec` runs.

The Release-EXE workflow runs `pyinstaller easy_tdx.spec --noconfirm` after `pip install -e .[web,packaging]` and the frontend build, so this spec is the canonical builder entry-point for the Windows EXE.
The README's '示例文件' table maps `finance_info.py` to 'Python API · 最新财务快照（37 字段）' — it cross-references the file and summarizes the output.
README's table maps `company_info.py` to 'Python API · F10 板块目录 + 遍历各板块正文'; also referenced in the 'Python API 快速开始' snippet.
README's table maps `company_cli.sh` to 'CLI · finance-info / company-info 全用法'; the field-reference section also documents the CLI's table / JSON / CSV output switches.
README's table maps `company_web_api.py` to 'Web API · /finance /company/category /company/content'; the Web API 快速开始 block lists the same three curl commands.
`_get_version()` calls `importlib.metadata.version('easy-tdx')` which reads from the installed package's METADATA — needs the package to be importable (editable install) at sphinx-build time.
Both 01_connection examples feed the same `best_host` selection flow — `ping_servers.py` shows the raw ping-and-rank, `connect_best_host.py` shows the same logic wrapped in `from_best_host()`.
Twin scripts with identical DataFrame shape — `history_minute_data.py` takes a YYYYMMDD date arg; `minute_time_data.py` is the current-day no-arg variant.
Twin scripts with identical schema — `history_transaction.py` takes a YYYYMMDD date arg; `transaction_data.py` is the current-day variant.

`security_list_all.py` is the cross-market, industry-mapped superset of `security_list.py` — the docstring of `security_list.py` explicitly tells users to switch to `get_security_list_all()` when industry columns are needed.
`index_bars.py` is the index-specific variant of `security_bars.py` — both share the KlineCategory schema, but indices get a dedicated 通达信 command and report aggregate vol/amount.

`get_block_info` is a method on the `TdxClient` class defined in src/easy_tdx/client.py.
Mirror of fund_flow.py: same 8-column schema; history version paginates by `start`/`count` and documents the Category-22 ↔ tick-recompute fallback path.

The AVAILABLE_FILES loop writes `block_gn.dat` to OUTPUT_DIR.

Identical format and purpose — `read_history_financial()` parses both the shipped gpcw20260331.dat and any locally-stored gpcw*.dat; the gpcw.dat on disk IS one of the candidate files this example searches for.

Both 11_mac_quotes examples use the same MacClient 7709 protocol + identical 12-column DataFrame schema; stock_quotes.py is the per-code batched variant, quotes_list.py is the category-sorted variant.
Twin examples: tick_chart.py is the single-day intraday; multi_day_tick.py is the multi-day variant with the same MacTick columns.
Demonstrates the same `get_block_info` API at the file-download level (saves block_gn.dat to disk) that block_info.py consumes at the runtime-call level.
block_info.py is the online-network fetch variant; block_data.py is the offline-dat-file variant of the same `TdxBlock` / `CustomerBlock` data — preferred when TDX_HOME is set.

The two scripts cover the same historical-financial data: report_file.py pulls fresh via `TdxClient.get_financial_records()` from CALC_HOSTS and saves the .zip; history_financial.py is the offline reader for whatever gpcw*.dat or gpcw*.zip it finds on disk.

Demonstrates every public function re-exported by easy_tdx.offline (`detect_tdx_home`, `resolve_vipdoc`, `find_daily_bar_file`, `find_5min_bar_file`, `find_lc1_bar_file`) — the script is the user's tour of the offline API surface.
Both demonstrate `easy_tdx.offline` reading off-disk binary-bar files for a security; daily_bars.py is daily OHLCV while min_bars.py covers the three minute-bar formats (.5 / .lc1 / .lc5).
Both are offline daily-bar readers; daily_bars.py targets `vipdoc/{sh,sz}/lday/*.day` (A-share, integer prices), ex_daily_bars.py targets `vipdoc/ds/lday/*.day` (extended market, IEEE-754 floats).
kline_offset.py exposes the server-side K-line total available; stock_kline.py is the data-fetching cousin on the same MacClient 7709 protocol.
quotes_list.py is the category-sorted variant (A股 top-N by 涨幅%); stock_quotes.py is the per-code batched variant — both share the same 7709 MacClient protocol and 12-column DataFrame schema.
Both files are siblings in examples/15_mac_board/ — board_list.py fetches the board universe, board_members.py drills into one board's constituents; together they form the board exploration workflow.
Siblings in examples/15_mac_board/. board_list provides board codes that board_summary aggregates (member_count/amount/main_net).
Both consume BoardType but board_ranking aggregates cross-board metrics (change_pct / main_net_amount) while board_list returns per-board info. Sister scripts.

board_summary internally calls get_board_members() to fetch all constituent quotes and then aggregates them into member_count/amount/vol/main_net_* — same MAC-protocol pagination, different aggregation level.
Both files demonstrate MAC-protocol single-stock-vs-aggregated-data patterns; capital_flow is per-symbol while board_members is per-board-constituent.
server_info documents that 09:15-09:25 is the call-auction window with type=5/6/7 phases; auction.py fetches the 09:15-09:25 matching data within that window — semantically paired.
Both fetch per-symbol intraday-data on MAC protocol: auction is 09:15-09:25 matching, symbol_info is full-day snapshot — same market, different time windows.
Both consume MAC-protocol market-monitoring data; server_info returns session metadata while unusual returns per-alert event stream. Different purposes but same protocol family.
Both demonstrate MAC-protocol pagination (600 for unusual, 80 for board_members) with start-offset loop accumulation — same pagination idiom across different protocol commands.

Sister scripts in examples/18_mac_ex/. goods_list returns the universe, goods_quotes returns real-time quotes for a subset of that universe — same extension-market MacExClient, different protocol commands.
Both fetch time-series K-line data via MacExClient on extension markets (HK/US/Futures); ex_kline returns daily bars while ex_tick_chart returns intraday minute bars.
Both fetch real-time HK/US data via MacExClient: ex_quotes gets snapshot quotes, ex_tick_chart gets intraday minute ticks. Same market, different time granularity.
UnifiedTdxClient proxies get_board_list() to its MacClient sub-component — same MAC-protocol 0x... call path as board_list.py uses directly.
UnifiedTdxClient proxies goods_kline() to its MacExClient sub-component (port 7727); ex_kline.py calls that same goods_kline API directly. They share the same extension-market K-line data path.

cli_examples.sh section 23 (`easy-tdx ex tick HK_MAIN_BOARD 00700`) is the CLI surface for the same MacExClient.goods_tick_chart() Python call used in ex_tick_chart.py.

cli_examples.sh sections 24-29 (`easy-tdx indicator-list / indicator MACD / indicator MACD,KDJ,RSI,BOLL / --params / --no-ohlcv / --period 5MIN`) are the CLI surface for the same get_stock_kline_with_indicators + compute_indicators Python calls used in basic_indicators.py.
cli_examples.sh section 24 (`easy-tdx indicator-list --table`) is the CLI surface for the same list_indicators() Python call used in list_indicators.py.

Sibling files in examples/21_indicator/. list_indicators enumerates the 30 supported indicator names/metadata; basic_indicators shows actual usage of a 4-indicator subset (MACD/KDJ/RSI/BOLL) + standalone compute_indicators for ATR/CCI/WR.
Both files use MA from easy_tdx.MyTT (basic_indicators via indicator engine, backtest_601088 via self.I in DualMAStrategy.init). Shared MyTT indicator foundation.

main() instantiates `AttributionAnalyzer(r_c.trades, r_c.equity_curve)` and calls `att.cost_attribution()` — attribution.py provides the AttributionReport.total_return/total_trade_cost/commission_cost/slippage_cost/stamp_tax_cost that gets printed.

Imports `TWAPExecution` from easy_tdx.backtest.execution and passes it to BacktestEngine as `execution_model=TWAPExecution(n_bars=3)` — execution.py defines ImmediateExecution / TWAPExecution / VWAPExecution / LimitExecution.

Imports `PercentSlippage, SquareRootSlippage` from easy_tdx.backtest.slippage and uses them as `slippage_model=PercentSlippage(rate=0.001)` and `SquareRootSlippage(impact_coeff=0.1)` — slippage.py defines FixedSlippage / PercentSlippage / SquareRootSlippage / VolumeSlippage.

DualMAStrategy subclasses Strategy and overrides init/next using `self.I(MA, self.data.close, N)` and `self.position["size"]` / `self.buy(size, stop_loss, take_profit)` / `self.sell(size)` — strategy.py defines the Strategy base class + StrategyDataProxy.

DualMAStrategy.init uses `from easy_tdx.MyTT import MA` and calls `self.I(MA, self.data.close, 10)` / `self.I(MA, self.data.close, 30)` — MyTT.py provides the MA indicator function used by the indicator engine.

main() uses `TdxClient().get_security_bars(MARKET, CODE, KlineCategory.DAY, 0, 800)` to fetch the 800 daily bars that all 3 backtest variants share — client.py provides TdxClient (the standard TDX TCP-protocol client on port 7709).
DualMAStrategy explicitly subclasses Strategy from easy_tdx.backtest.strategy, overriding init() and next() — this is the canonical Strategy subclass contract.

main() instantiates BacktestEngine 3 times (no-friction / PercentSlippage / SquareRootSlippage+TWAPExecution) and calls .run(df) — engine.py's BacktestEngine.run() is the entry point for each backtest.
main() calls `AttributionAnalyzer(r_c.trades, r_c.equity_curve).cost_attribution()` to break down the highest-precision run's cost into commission/slippage/stamp_tax.

README.md's '### Python API' section gives the canonical Python snippet `StrengthRanker(preset='steady').rank(top_n=20)` that strength_api.py implements with 6 detailed variations (steady / breakout / custom / parallel-balanced / JSON output / programmatic access).

README.md's '### CLI' section gives 3 example `easy-tdx screen strength --preset steady --top 50 --table` commands that strength_cli.sh expands into 10 detailed flag combinations (preset/custom-weights/--workers/--min-amount/--universe/--min-listed-days/--output).

README.md's '### Web API' section documents the `curl 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1/market/strength?preset=breakout&top_n=20'` call that strength_web_api.py implements as 4 HTTP GET requests via Python requests.

README.md documents the `StrengthRanker` class API (`ranker.rank(top_n=20)`, `preset=`, output `r.rank/r.code/r.strength/...`), the `STRENGTH_PRESETS` dict (steady/breakout/balanced), and the `strength = (w5·ret_5 + ...)/vol_20` formula — all implemented in `easy_tdx.screen.strength`.

Imports `STRENGTH_PRESETS, StrengthRanker` from easy_tdx.screen.strength and instantiates StrengthRanker 4 times (steady / breakout / custom / parallel-balanced) with various w5/w20/w60/vol_adjusted/min_amount combinations.

All 4 HTTP GET requests target `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/market/strength` — this is the FastAPI router endpoint implemented in easy_tdx.web.routers.market that internally delegates to StrengthRanker.rank().
main() instantiates 4 StrengthRanker objects (steady/breakout/custom/balanced) and calls .rank(top_n=..., workers=..., progress_callback=...) and the formatters .to_table() + .to_json().
fetch_strength() issues `requests.get('/api/v1/market/strength', params={preset, top_n, universe, min_amount}, timeout=120)` — direct invocation of the FastAPI router endpoint.
fetch_strength_custom_weights() passes w5/w20/w60 overrides via query params — the FastAPI endpoint must support these per-param overrides in addition to preset mode.
NOTICE states the initial project structure and online protocol implementation were based on xmtdx and have been substantially rewritten — the online protocol client lives in easy_tdx.client.
run_all() iterates `BacktestEngine(strategy=strategy_cls, cash=cash, commission=commission).run(df)` once per discovered strategy file — engine.py is the core dependency.

run_all() checks `isinstance(obj, type) and issubclass(obj, Strategy)` to detect strategy classes via dynamic importlib loading — strategy.py defines the Strategy base class used as the duck-type filter.

_run_combo_screen() imports `CombinationRunner` from easy_tdx.backtest.combo and uses `runner.screen(combo_sizes=(size,), mode=combo_mode.upper())` — combo.py implements CombinationRunner + the AND/OR/MAJORITY signal merge.
run_all() calls `parse_market(market)`, `parse_period(period)`, `parse_adjust(adjust)` from easy_tdx.cli.parsers — parsers.py converts CLI string args to typed enums.

run_all() uses `MacClient.from_best_host()` to fetch the bar data (with `client.get_stock_kline(mkt, code, period=..., start=0, count=count, adjust=...)`) and optionally `client.get_stock_quotes([(mkt, code)])` for the stock name when --show is enabled.

run_all() itself does not import MyTT, but it loads Strategy subclasses from strategies/*.py which typically use MyTT MA/MACD/KDJ etc. via self.I(MA, ...). The strategies directory's expected usage of MyTT is implicit in this driver.
run_all() loops over strategy_files and calls `engine = BacktestEngine(strategy=strategy_cls, cash=cash, commission=commission)` then `result = engine.run(df)` for each — direct BacktestEngine usage.

_run_combo_screen() instantiates `CombinationRunner(strategy_classes=strategy_classes, df=df, cash=cash, commission=commission)` and calls `runner.screen(combo_sizes=(size,), mode=combo_mode.upper())` — direct CombinationRunner.screen() usage.

Both files use matplotlib twinx to render normalized stock-price + equity-curve overlays with buy/sell markers — same chart idiom. backtest_601088 prints one strategy's equity, run_all prints the best strategy's equity.

Both files are backtest demos that run Strategy subclasses and print ranked performance — backtest_601088 runs 3 variants of one strategy with different execution/slippage models, run_all runs N strategies with default model. Same orchestration pattern.

Both files consume the strength-ranker data; strength_api calls StrengthRanker.rank() directly via Python, strength_web_api calls /api/v1/market/strength via HTTP. Two layers of the same data pipeline.
Both demonstrate the screen-strength feature; strength_cli.sh is the bash/CLI surface, strength_api.py is the Python surface. Same underlying StrengthRanker.

layers.name: Protocol Codec Layer
layers.description: Low-level binary encoders/decoders for the 通达信 (TongDaXin) TCP wire protocol. Handles the 16-byte frame header (magic=0x0074CBB1, ZipFlag, SeqID, Method, zipsize, unzipsize), zlib body decompression, variable-length signed price encoding, 4-byte volume custom float format, and the 10-byte MAC protocol request frame. Pure parsing functions — no I/O.

layers.name: Protocol Command Layer
layers.description: One BaseCommand subclass per TDX protocol command (GetSecurityCountCmd, GetSecurityBarsCmd, GetTransactionDataCmd, GetXdxrInfoCmd, etc.). Each class encapsulates build_request (bytes out) and parse_response (dict/list in). Pure data shape — transport layer feeds them bytes. Covers the full A-share, HK, index, fund-flow, finance, and minute-data command surface.

layers.name: Transport Layer
layers.description: TCP connection lifecycle: handshake setup bytes (3 commands), frame send/receive, zlib body inflation, reconnect/backoff, best-host selection across known TDX server IPs, async transport wrapper, and the extended-market (MAC) transport on port 7727. Glues the command classes to the socket. Includes the failed-host tracking that powers the v1.20.0 auto-ping failover.

layers.name: Domain Core Layer
layers.description: Public client API and high-level domain models: easy_tdx.TdxClient (sync), easy_tdx.AsyncTdxClient (async), easy_tdx.unified (market-info / quotes / kline / tick / transaction / auction / capital-flow), easy_tdx.MyTT (technical-indicator toolkit — 60+ indicators built on pure Python numpy-free math), easy_tdx.indicator (high-level indicator wrappers), chanlun (缠论) analysis, screen/strength screening, cninfo + sina data fallbacks, and the DataFrame proxy.

layers.name: MAC Protocol Extension Layer
layers.description: Alternative MAC protocol (port 7727) for futures/HK/foreign markets. Includes 30+ commands under src/easy_tdx/mac/commands/, the MAC transport, qfq adjustment (adjust.py), tick charts, board info, capital flow, and ex_* commands for HK stocks. Has its own command base class and its own frame convention (10-byte header with head_flag=0x1c).

layers.name: FastAPI Web Server Layer
layers.description: FastAPI application exposing the easy-tdx library as REST endpoints (src/easy_tdx/web/). Includes the app factory, router modules (bars, block, board_mac, chanlun, finance, ex_market, ...), service layer (factor_service.py, portfolio_service.py), request validation via Pydantic schemas, CORS, and the system-tray icon launcher (src/easy_tdx/tray.py with pystray + Pillow). Designed for `easy-tdx web` and `pip install easy-tdx[web]` consumers.

layers.name: Backtest Engine Layer
layers.description: Backtesting framework: Strategy ABC, OrderSimulator, PerformanceAnalyzer, slippage models (fixed/percentage/square-root/volume), execution models (immediate/TWAP/VWAP/limit), Brinson + factor + transaction-cost attribution, portfolio rebalance + optimizer, factor engine + scoring, and a strategy registry. Drives `easy-tdx backtest` CLI subcommand. The 18 built-in strategies in strategies/*.py are thin Strategy subclasses built on MyTT indicators.

layers.name: Offline Data Layer
layers.description: Offline / cached data sources that don't hit live TDX servers. Includes src/easy_tdx/offline/ (loader, storage, cache, factory, index, calendar, converter, financial, downloader, validators) for historical data without the live TCP path. Reads gpcw.txt + gpcw*.zip financial files via codec/financial.py and tdxhy.cfg industry config via codec/industry.py.

layers.name: CLI Entry Layer
layers.description: Click-based CLI dispatcher (src/easy_tdx/cli/). One cmd_<feature>.py per CLI subcommand: market info, quotes, kline, tick, transaction, auction, board, capital-flow, unusual, server info, symbol info, chanlun, backtest, optimize, and admin. Registered through easy_tdx.cli:cli (pyproject.toml entry point).

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: All test files under tests/unit/ (~64 files, mostly pytest with pytest-asyncio). Includes offline decoder tests (test_commands_offline.py), codec unit tests (test_codec_*.py), live-client tests with mocked servers, chanlun algorithm tests, MAC protocol integration tests, backtest engine tests, factor engine tests, portfolio tests, and optimization tests. Tests/fixtures/ contains paired .hex (raw bytes) + .json (expected values) goldens.

layers.name: Vue 3 Frontend Layer
layers.description: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite web UI (web-ui/src/). Pinia stores (backtest store), vue-router, 17 SFC components (charts via ECharts: KlineChart, EquityChart, CompareChart; tables: MetricTable, CompareTable; badges: GradeBadge; heatmaps: OptimizeHeatmap), 7 view pages (BacktestView, OptimizeView, CompareView, plus grading/mac views), and types definitions. Built into web-ui/dist/ which the hatchling force-include ships into the wheel.

layers.name: Examples & Built-in Strategies
layers.description: User-facing example scripts organized by feature area (01_connection, 02_market_info, 03_kline, ..., 23_screen_strength). Plus the 18 built-in trading strategies under strategies/*.py — each a Strategy subclass using MyTT indicators. These are the canonical examples users copy to start writing their own.

layers.name: CI/CD & Release Layer
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows (.github/workflows/): ci.yml (matrix test on Ubuntu+Windows × Python 3.10/3.12/3.13 + mypy + frontend build), publish.yml (PyPI trusted publishing + sigstore), release.yml (PyInstaller Windows EXE → GitHub Release). Plus readthedocs.yaml, easy_tdx.spec (PyInstaller spec), pyproject.toml (hatchling backend with web-ui/dist force-include), and requirements-dev.txt.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: User-facing Markdown docs (docs/*.md) — API reference, backtest usage, quickstart, installation, field mapping, indicator guides (BIAS, ZHUOYAO), protocol reverse-engineering notes, and internal design specs/plans under docs/superpowers/. Plus the top-level README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and LICENSE/NOTICE.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: easy-tdx is an open-source Python library for accessing 通达信 (TongDaXin) market data over TCP. It includes a Python library at src/easy_tdx/, a Vue3 + TypeScript web UI in web-ui/, a FastAPI server, a Click CLI, 18 built-in trading strategies, and a backtesting engine. Start with the README to understand the project's purpose.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: The package is wired up via the easy_tdx.cli:cli Click group (pyproject.toml entry point). Each cmd_*.py file is one CLI subcommand — 25+ subcommands cover market info, quotes, kline, tick, transaction, backtest, chanlun, optimize, and admin.

tour.title: Protocol Codec Layer
tour.description: The lowest layer — pure binary encoders/decoders for the TDX TCP protocol. The 16-byte response frame (magic=0x0074CBB1), zlib body inflation, the variable-length signed price encoding, the 4-byte custom volume float, the MAC protocol 10-byte request frame, the financial-data .dat binary format, the industry-config .cfg parser, and the A-share price-limit rules engine. No I/O lives here.

tour.title: Protocol Command Layer
tour.description: One BaseCommand subclass per TDX protocol command. Each class encapsulates build_request (bytes out) and parse_response (dict/list in). Pure data shape — transport layer feeds them bytes. ~20 commands cover A-share quotes, K-line, transaction, finance, fund-flow, xdxr, company-info, minute data, and report-file download.

tour.title: Transport Layer
tour.description: TCP connection lifecycle: handshake, frame send/receive, zlib decompression, reconnect/backoff, best-host selection, async transport. The v1.20.0 auto-ping failover lives here — tracks failed hosts and switches to a fresh server without manual intervention. Includes ex/transport.py for the alternative MAC protocol on port 7727.

tour.title: Domain Core & Public API
tour.description: The single entry point most users touch: TdxClient (sync), AsyncTdxClient (async), the unified() facade for market info / quotes / kline / tick / transaction, MyTT (60+ technical indicators in pure Python), and the DataFrame proxy. Also exposes chanlun (缠论) analysis and the high-level realtime server.

tour.title: MAC Protocol Extension
tour.description: Alternative MAC protocol on port 7727 for futures / HK / foreign markets. Has its own command base class and frame convention (10-byte header with head_flag=0x1c). 22+ commands under src/easy_tdx/mac/commands/. Includes qfq adjustment, tick charts, capital flow, board info, auction, and ex_* commands for HK stocks.

tour.title: Backtest Engine
tour.description: Backtesting framework driven by `easy-tdx backtest`. Strategy ABC, OrderSimulator, PerformanceAnalyzer, slippage models (fixed/percentage/square-root/volume), execution models (immediate/TWAP/VWAP/limit), Brinson + factor + transaction-cost attribution. The 18 built-in strategies under strategies/*.py are thin Strategy subclasses built on MyTT.

tour.title: FastAPI Web Server
tour.description: FastAPI application exposing the library as REST endpoints. app.py handles bootstrap; the routers/ subdirectory contains 20+ feature routers (bars, block, board_mac, chanlun, finance, ex_market, etc.) backed by service modules. Designed for `easy-tdx web` and `pip install easy-tdx[web]` consumers.

tour.title: Vue 3 Frontend
tour.description: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite web UI. Pinia stores, vue-router, SFC components (charts via ECharts: KlineChart, EquityChart, CompareChart; tables: MetricTable, CompareTable; badges: GradeBadge; heatmaps: OptimizeHeatmap), and view pages (BacktestView, OptimizeView, CompareView, plus grading/mac views). Built into web-ui/dist/ which the hatchling force-include ships into the wheel — frontend must be built before pip install.

tour.title: Built-in Trading Strategies
tour.description: 18 canonical strategies users copy as templates. Categories: trend-following (ma_cross, expma_cross, macd_cross, trix_cross, dmi_trend, turtle_breakout), reversal/oscillator (rsi_reversal, kdj_golden, bias_reversal, bollinger_breakout, cci_breakout), volume-based (mfi_volume, obv_trend, volume_price), momentum (mtm_momentum), and multi-timeframe (zhuoyao_momentum). Each is a Strategy subclass using MyTT indicators.

tour.title: CI/CD & Release
tour.description: Three GitHub Actions workflows: ci.yml (matrix test on Ubuntu+Windows × Python 3.10/3.12/3.13 + mypy + frontend build), publish.yml (PyPI trusted publishing + sigstore via OIDC), release.yml (PyInstaller Windows EXE → GitHub Release). Plus readthedocs.yaml, easy_tdx.spec (PyInstaller spec), and pyproject.toml (hatchling backend with web-ui/dist force-include).
